We’ll travel to Lower Saxony and visit Wolfsburg, a city founded in 1938 as a planned settlement for the workers of the Volkswagen factory during the Nazi era. The entire city revolves around the German automobile giant. We’ll include admission to Autostadt, the city of automobiles, featuring gardens and exhibitions. You’ll have time for a walk and lunch. We’ll then continue to Celle, where you can stroll through its old town, known for its half-timbered houses from the 16th and 17th centuries, as well as its imposing Renaissance castle. We’ll arrive in Hannover in the mid-afternoon, where you can explore the capital of Lower Saxony, an important economic, cultural, and scientific hub in the country. Its architecture blends historic and modern buildings, along with extensive parks. Accommodation will be here.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Berlin","to":"Hanover","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":693,"h_name":"MARITIM AIRPORT HOTEL HANNOVER","h_country":"Germany","h_city":"Hanover","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Hanover/MARITIM AIRPORT HOTEL HANNOVER/1741156262430-2024-06-27.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Hanover/MARITIM AIRPORT HOTEL HANNOVER/1741156262511-BR_LHDO_04_Sep20_2400x1600.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Hanover/MARITIM AIRPORT HOTEL HANNOVER/1741156262627-2020-11-16.jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Eisenach","country":"Germany","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Hanover to Eisenach","day_summary":"

Begin your adventure in Hanover, the elegant city of royal gardens and grand boulevards, where history and modern life blend seamlessly. Stroll through the enchanting Herrenhausen Gardens, marvel at the striking New Town Hall, and soak in the vibrant atmosphere of Lower Saxony’s capital.


From there, travel south through Germany’s picturesque landscapes—rolling hills, lush forests, and charming half-timbered villages—on your way to the historic town of Eisenach. Nestled at the foot of the Thuringian Forest, Eisenach welcomes you with stories straight out of history books. Explore the legendary Wartburg Castle, a UNESCO World Heritage Site where Martin Luther translated the New Testament, and wander the streets where Johann Sebastian Bach once lived.


This journey from Hanover to Eisenach is not just a passage between two cities—it's a deep dive into Germany’s rich cultural tapestry, filled with royal legacies, revolutionary ideas, and musical genius. Whether you're a history lover, a culture enthusiast, or simply a traveler at heart, this unforgettable tour promises a perfect blend of discovery and inspiration.

We’ll start the day by visiting the impressive Wartburg Castle (tickets included), a UNESCO World Heritage site where Martin Luther translated the New Testament into German. Afterward, in Eisenach, we’ll visit the Automobile Museum (admission included), located in the former main factory of East Germany´s automobile industry, where we’ll see a collection of vintage cars. You’ll then have some free time in the city to explore and enjoy lunch, and if you’re a music lover, you might want to visit the Bach House. Lunch will be included.


Our journey will continue to Gotha, where we’ll admire its grand baroque palace (exterior visit) and charming historic town center. In Erfurt, the capital of Thuringia, we’ll discover a vibrant city featuring a magnificent cathedral and a medieval old town, including a beautiful bridge that has been inhabited for over 500 years.


By late afternoon, we’ll arrive in Leipzig, one of Germany´s most historically and culturally rich cities. Home to famous composers like Bach and Wagner, Leipzig was also a major industrial center during the German Democratic Republic era and today has a population of over half a million.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Eisenach","to":"Leipzig","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":857,"h_name":"GRONERS Leipzig","h_country":"Germany","h_city":"Leipzig","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Leipzig/GRONERS Leipzig/1744805047833-GRONERS Leipzig 2.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Leipzig/GRONERS Leipzig/1744805047952-GRONERS Leipzig.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Leipzig/GRONERS Leipzig/1744805047959-GRONERS Leipzig 1.jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Prague","country":"Czech Republic","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Czech Republic/Prague/1740991308703-istockphoto-956087480-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Czech Republic/Prague/1740991308789-istockphoto-1097810660-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Czech Republic/Prague/1740991308832-istockphoto-628365610-612x612.jpg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Leipzig to Prague","day_summary":"

Leaving Leipzig, we’ll stop at the Monument to the Battle of the Nations, inaugurated in 1913 and standing 91 meters tall, commemorating a famous battle from the Napoleonic Wars. We’ll then continue to Dresden, the capital of Saxony, known for its Baroque and Renaissance architecture, stunning palaces, and scenic promenades along the Elbe River. You’ll have time to enjoy lunch and explore the city.


Later, we’ll cross into the Czech Republic. Arriving in Prague by mid-afternoon, we’ll include a panoramic tour of this beautiful city, often regarded as one of the most enchanting in Europe. Highlights include Prague Castle, the Old Town Square, and the stunning Charles Bridge. To finish the day, we’ll offer an evening transfer to the city center, where you will find many traditional breweries.

A free day to explore Prague at your own pace. Alternatively, you will have the option to explore Artistic Prague, including Mala Strana, the Infant Jesus of Prague, St. Nicholas Church, a cruise on the Vltava River, and the Jewish Quarter.

Today, dive into the heart of Europe with Travelxploria as we explore Prague, the Baroque jewel of the continent. From the moment of pick-up, our experienced Travelxploria tour guide and on-the-ground support team will ensure a seamless and enriching experience. Enjoy a comprehensive panoramic tour of the city, where you’ll discover Prague’s most iconic monuments, including the breathtaking Prague Castle (admission included). Our local guide will share captivating stories and historical insights, bringing the city to life. Afterward, unwind with a relaxing boat ride along the Vltava River, offering stunning views of the city’s skyline. In the afternoon, you’ll have free time to wander through Prague’s charming streets at your own pace. Throughout the day, our dedicated Travelxploria representative will be by your side, ensuring you feel supported, with smooth logistics for a stress-free experience

We’ll depart early from Prague and travel to the Moravia region, where we’ll visit Lednice, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. We’ll have time to explore its beautiful gardens and admire its stunning castle. Crossing through Slovakia, we’ll enter Hungary and make a stop in Gyor, the city of four rivers, where we’ll have time for lunch and a leisurely stroll. Our journey will continue to Budapest, arriving in the mid-afternoon. In the evening, we’ll include a transfer to the pedestrian area, where you’ll find charming traditional restaurants, many of them featuring live music.

Buda Castle, Fisherman's Bastion, Matthias Church, and St. Stephen’s Basilica. A tour through the heart of Budapest lets you discover both the Buda and Pest sides of the city. The guide will walk you through key historical landmarks, such as the majestic Buda Castle, the stunning Fisherman's Bastion, and the iconic Matthias Church. You’ll also visit the beautiful St. Stephen’s Basilica in Pest, which is home to Hungary’s most revered relic.

We’ll depart early from Budapest and travel through Slovakia, where we’ll visit Bratislava, the country’s capital on the banks of the Danube. There will be time for a stroll through its historic center, nestled at the foot of the imposing castle.


Our journey will then take us to Trencin, a charming medieval town in western Slovakia, known for its impressive castle and vibrant historic center. There will be time to explore and enjoy lunch.


In the afternoon, we’ll continue our route through picturesque landscapes of forests and rolling hills as we make our way to Poland. We’ll arrive in Krakow at the end of the day.

On your journey from Krakow to Warsaw, you'll make two meaningful stops. First, in Wadowice, the birthplace of Pope John Paul II, you’ll visit the Pope's childhood home, now a museum, and the Basilica of the Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary. This peaceful town offers a chance to reflect on the life of a man who left a profound mark on both Poland and the world. Next, you’ll visit the Auschwitz-Birkenau Memorial and Museum, a sobering and powerful site that commemorates the horrors of the Holocaust. Walking through the preserved barracks and exhibitions, you'll gain a deeper understanding of the atrocities that took place there. After these reflective stops, you'll continue your journey to Warsaw, Poland's resilient capital, where you’ll arrive in the evening to settle in and prepare for the next part of your adventure.

In the morning, we’ll enjoy a panoramic tour of Warsaw with a local guide. This city of nearly two million people, Poland’s political and economic hub, is both classic and modern, traditional and cosmopolitan, continually reborn. We’ll explore the charming Old Town, with its historic buildings, Royal Palace, and cobblestone streets, all beautifully restored after their destruction in World War II and now recognized as a World Heritage Site. We’ll also visit the iconic Palace of Culture, a skyscraper gifted by the Soviet Union, reflect on the former largest Jewish ghetto in Europe, and stroll through Lazienkowski Park, home to the Chopin Monument. At around 2:30 p.m., after some time for a walk and lunch, we’ll depart for Pomerania until we reach our next stop: Poznan, an active city with a magnificent main square and vibrant atmosphere. You’ll have some time to explore.

packages.package_name: Grand Tour of Central Europe
packages.overview:
• Germany – Explore the grandeur of Berlin’s Brandenburg Gate, the fairytale Neuschwanstein Castle, and the historic charm of Munich and Frankfurt.
  • Austria – Discover Vienna’s Schönbrunn Palace, the music-filled streets of Salzburg, and the picturesque beauty of Innsbruck in the heart of the Alps.
  • Czech Republic – Walk through the cobbled streets of Prague, marvel at Charles Bridge and Prague Castle, and soak in the old-world charm of this medieval capital.
  • Hungary – Enjoy the elegance of Budapest, with its stunning Parliament Building, Buda Castle, and relaxing thermal baths.
  • Poland – Visit the historic streets of Kraków and Warsaw, experience the poignant history of Auschwitz, and admire the beauty of Wrocław’s market square.
  • Slovakia – Explore the Bratislava Castle, walk along the Danube River, and discover the medieval charm of Slovakia’s capital.

Welcome to Rome. As you arrive, our Travelxploria representative will be there to warmly greet you and transfer you to your hotel. Take some time to relax and settle in. Later, our guide will personally meet you to share all the essential details about your journey. You can also check the hotel reception area for important updates.

In the evening, we’ll take you to Via Cavour, a lively street perfect for your first taste of Rome. Explore its vibrant atmosphere and choose from a variety of restaurants

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Rome Fiumicino Airport","to":"Rome","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":661,"h_name":"Ergife Palace Hotel","h_country":"Italy","h_city":"Rome","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Rome/Ergife Palace Hotel/1741088856129-2024-05-31.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Rome/Ergife Palace Hotel/1741088856238-unnamed (19).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Rome/Ergife Palace Hotel/1741088856244-unnamed (18).jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Rome City Tour","day_summary":"

In the morning, we’ll enjoy a panoramic tour of Italy’s capital, the Eternal City, one of the oldest and most influential cities in Western civilization. We’ll combine a walking tour with a panoramic bus ride. During the walking tour, we´ll admire the Arch of Constantine, the Colosseum (from the outside), and the impressive Imperial Forums. After that, we’ll continue by bus, exploring this open-air-museum city with majestic basilicas and churches, passing by Tiber Island, Lungotevere Avenue, and Castel Sant´Angelo. The tour will finish at Vatican City, an independent state within Rome and the center of the Catholic Church. The afternoon will be free. We recommend visiting St. Peter’s Basilica and the Vatican Museums.

In the evening, we’ll provide a transfer to Trastevere, perfect for a leisurely stroll through its charming, narrow cobblestone streets. This district is rich in hidden gems, from quaint medieval churches and quirky shops selling unique items to everyday scenes of local life. You’ll find many trattorias (casual eating establishments, typically less formal than a ristorante) and osterias (similar to taverns), each exuding Italian charm and ready to serve you authentic local dishes. Additionally, the area offers a variety of restaurants serving Indian, Chinese, and international cuisine. We’ll return to the hotel at the end of the evening.

Join Travelxploria’s expert guide for an unforgettable journey through the heart of Rome, where history, art, and culture come alive. Starting from a central meeting point, our best-in-class support staff ensures a smooth experience as you explore iconic landmarks like the Colosseum, Roman Forum, Pantheon, and Trevi Fountain. Stroll through Piazza Navona, soak in the grandeur of St. Peter’s Basilica, and uncover the city’s hidden gems. With Travelxploria’s top-notch service, skip the hassle and embrace the beauty of Rome’s timeless charm. Let us craft the perfect adventure, ensuring the best experience of your Roman holiday!

Today, we’ll leave Rome and head north, taking in the beautiful landscapes along the way. Upon reaching Florence, we’ll go directly to the stunning Piazzale Michelangelo for breathtaking views of the city. In the evening, we’ll enjoy a guided walking tour with a local expert through the historic center, visiting landmarks such as the Ponte Vecchio, the Duomo, and Piazza della Signoria. This city is like an open-air museum. Afterward, you´ll have some free time to explore at your leisure. In the evening, you’ll have plenty of options for dinner, as this district offers many restaurants with a variety of international cuisines.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Rome","to":"Florence","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":678,"h_name":"Novotel Firenze Nord Aeroporto","h_country":"Italy","h_city":"Florence","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Florence/Novotel Firenze Nord Aeroporto/1741152956443-2024-07-01 (2).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Florence/Novotel Firenze Nord Aeroporto/1741152956518-unnamed (40).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Florence/Novotel Firenze Nord Aeroporto/1741152956623-2023-01-18.jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Venice","country":"Italy","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Italy/Venice/1741006336475-istockphoto-1200916277-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Italy/Venice/1741006336571-istockphoto-1388018793-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Italy/Venice/1741006336619-istockphoto-911570904-612x612.jpg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Florence to Venice","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll enjoy the stunning mountain landscapes as we cross the Apennines. Upon arriving in Venice, we’ll take a boat across the Giudecca Canal to the Island of Venice. With a local guide, we’ll visit the marvelous St. Mark’s Square. Afterwards, we’ll tour the Murano Glass Factory, where you’ll see the fascinating techniques used to create this ancient glass. You´ll have some free time to explore on your own. Accommodation will be in the Mestre district.

We will depart from Venice, traveling to Austria! Upon arriving in Innsbruck, you’ll have time to stroll through the historic city center and enjoy lunch in the Austrian Tyrolean capital. Afterward, we’ll journey along beautiful scenic roads through the mountains to Kitzbühel. Once there, you’ll have time to explore this picturesque village, a famous ski resort where snow can be enjoyed for most of the year. We’ll continue our journey to Salzburg, arriving by the end of the day.

Today, we’ll get to know Austria better. During the morning, we´ll explore Salzburg, Mozart’s city. Its Renaissance and Baroque center is filled with beauty, all in the shadow of the grand castle. Next, we’ll travel through lakes and mountains to reach St. Wolfgang, a renowned pilgrimage site in Austria. We’ll then head to Traunkirchen, located beside the beautiful Traunsee Lake, where we’ll take an included cruise to see four of the lake´s castles before disembarking in Gmunden. Afterward, we’ll continue to Vienna, arriving in the evening.

Today, we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of this impressive city with a local guide. We’ll explore its majestic avenues, beautiful palaces, large parks and vibrant city center. In the afternoon, you’ll have free time to explore on your own. In the evening, we’ll include a transfer to the Grinzing district, an old wine-growing village now part of the city, known for its traditional taverns. You’ll have free time to dine in the area.

After about three hours of travel, we’ll arrive in Budapest, the capital of Hungary. Our first stop will be the lively area of Váci utca, where you’ll have free time for lunch. After, we’ll kick off our exploration of Budapest with an included boat trip on the Danube River, with historic Buda on one bank and cosmopolitan Pest on the other. The views are unbeatable. Following this, we’ll begin a panoramic tour of the so-called \"Queen of the Danube.\" You’ll have some free time afterwards. In the evening, we’ll offer an optional outing to a Magyar show, with dinner included.

We will leave Budapest early and head for Bratislava, the capital of Slovakia, a charming city along the Danube with an attractive historic center. You´ll have time to explore and enjoy lunch. After, we’ll continue our journey to the Czech Republic, arriving in Prague in the evening. We’ll provide a transfer to the Old Town Square, a central spot that offers a fantastic introduction to Prague with its vibrant atmosphere and numerous tourist activities. You can have dinner at one of the many restaurants offering a variety of international cuisines, including Czech, Chinese, Indian, and Italian. Afterward, we’ll return to the hotel.

During the tour, there will be a visit to Prague Castle (admission included). After that, enjoy a boat ride on the Vltava River (included). We’ll depart early from Prague and stop in Pilsen, in western Bohemia, a city renowned for its beer. You’ll have time to stroll through its historic center, where you’ll find the Old Town Square, the cathedral, the Plague Column, and a Renaissance-era town hall. We’ll then cross into Germany and follow part of the Romantic Road. We’ll visit Rothenburg, a town that seems to come straight out of a fairy tale and is considered one of the most beautiful villages in the country. Afterward, we’ll head to Frankfurt, the financial capital of Germany. After our arrival in the evening, enjoy free time in the historic city center, where you’ll find a wide variety of restaurants offering Indian, Asian, Italian, and international cuisines.

packages.package_name: Timeless Europe A Tour of History Art and Nature
packages.overview:
• Frankfurt, Germany: A dynamic financial hub with a blend of modern skyscrapers and historic architecture. Visit the Römer, St. Bartholomew's Cathedral, and explore the charming old town (Altstadt).

  • Dortmund, Germany: Known for its football culture and industrial heritage, Dortmund offers attractions like the Signal Iduna Park, the German Football Museum, and the Westfalenpark with its stunning views.

  • Hanover, Germany: A city rich in history and culture, home to magnificent gardens like Herrenhausen Gardens, the Lower Saxony State Museum, and a lively arts scene.

  • Aarhus, Denmark: A city where the past meets the future, with attractions like the ARoS Aarhus Art Museum, the Old Town (Den Gamle By), and vibrant waterfront areas.

  • Stavanger, Norway: A coastal city famous for its stunning natural beauty, including the famous Preikestolen (Pulpit Rock). Explore its charming old town and fascinating Viking history.

  • Bergen, Norway: Nestled between mountains and fjords, Bergen offers picturesque streets, the UNESCO World Heritage-listed Bryggen, and access to stunning fjord cruises.

  • Oslo, Norway: Norway’s capital is known for its vibrant cultural scene, including the Viking Ship Museum, the Munch Museum, and the beautiful Oslofjord, offering a perfect mix of urban life and nature.

  • Stockholm, Sweden: A city spread across 14 islands, known for its beautiful architecture, world-class museums like the Vasa Museum, and vibrant district of Gamla Stan (Old Town).

  • Copenhagen, Denmark: Denmark’s capital is a mix of royal history, modern design, and Scandinavian charm. Must-visit attractions include Tivoli Gardens, Nyhavn, and the iconic Little Mermaid statue.

  • Berlin, Germany: A city that blends history and modernity, Berlin is known for its rich cultural heritage, the Berlin Wall remnants, Brandenburg Gate, Museum Island, and a vibrant nightlife scene.

Welcome to your incredible journey with Travelxploria. Upon arrival, our friendly representative will be there to greet you and ensure a smooth transfer to your hotel. Take some time to relax and soak in the excitement of the adventure ahead. In the afternoon, feel free to explore the surroundings or unwind at the hotel. Our representative will be available to assist you, and you’ll find important tour details conveniently displayed on the hotel’s information board.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Frankfurt Airport","to":"Frankfurt","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":690,"h_name":"MARITIM FRANKFURT","h_country":"Germany","h_city":"Frankfurt","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Frankfurt/MARITIM FRANKFURT/1741155474506-2022-07-04.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Frankfurt/MARITIM FRANKFURT/1741155474588-2023-01-26.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Frankfurt/MARITIM FRANKFURT/1741155474629-unnamed (53).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Dortmund","country":"Germany","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Departure from Frankfurt with Cruise on the Rhine & Dortmund Arrival","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll explore the Rhine and Moselle Valleys, two rivers full of history and dotted with charming villages. We´ll begin with a short cruise on the Rhine, between the villages of Rüdesheim and Sankt Goar, undoubtedly the most picturesque section of the river. Next, we’ll head to the Moselle River, stopping for lunch in Cochem, a picturesque village on the Rhine riverbank overlooked by its imposing castle, which dates back over a thousand years. Afterward, we’ll travel to Cologne, where you´ll have time to visit its impressive Gothic cathedral. Standing at 154 meters tall, it was the tallest building in the world until the late 19th century. You can also enjoy a walk along the Rhine River and through the lively city center before we continue to our hotel in Dortmund.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Frankfurt","to":"Dortmund","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":691,"h_name":"MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM","h_country":"Germany","h_city":"Dortmund","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Dortmund/MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM/1741155695447-LogoEingangMercureDortmundCentrum - komprimiert.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Dortmund/MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM/1741155695481-2024-10-15.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Dortmund/MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM/1741155695551-2024-11-19.jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Hanover","country":"Germany","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Dortmund to Hanover & Stay at Hanover","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll embark on a magical journey, reminiscing about our favorite childhood stories and movies as we travel through the towns that inspired the Brothers Grimm, now known as the Fairy Tale Route. Our first stop will be Trendelburg, a picturesque walled village where we’ll visit the large medieval castle and the famous Rapunzel Tower (admission included).


We’ll continue along scenic roads, crossing forests that will remind us of the tale of Hansel and Gretel. Our next stop will be Höxter, a vibrant town where we’ll enjoy lunch and visit Corvey Abbey, an ancient 9th-century monastery and World Heritage Site (admission included). We’ll then make a brief stop in Polle to photograph the exterior ruins of Cinderella’s Castle. Our journey will continue to Hamelin, renowned for the tale of “The Pied Piper of Hamelin”. After following the mouse tracks, we’ll proceed to Hanover, where we’ll have time to explore the city center and take a stroll next to the old town hall.

After breakfast, we’ll head to Hamburg, where we’ll see its lively port surrounding the lake. Following lunch, we’ll travel to Flensburg, a charming seaside village with a marina on the border with Denmark. Our journey will then continue along the Jutland peninsula until we reach Aarhus, Denmark’s second-largest city.

We’ll travel to Hirtshals and take a ferry across the North Sea to Norway, embarking on a three-hour voyage to Kristiansand. From there, we’ll continue along Norway’s stunning southern coastline. Our next stop will be the charming little town of Flekkefjord. We’ll then arrive in Stavanger, a picturesque town with a bustling port by the fjord.

Upon leaving the city, we’ll stop at the Swords in Rock monument, a tribute to the Viking era, before continuing our scenic route through tunnels, bridges, and some segments by boat along the fjord coast of Norway. One of these undersea tunnels is the deepest in the world. The two boat trips will make this part of the journey both relaxing and spectacular. We’ll arrive in Bergen at lunchtime and take a cable car up Mount Fløyen for amazing views of the town and the Bergen fjord. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore the capital city of fjords.

Today, we’ll experience more breathtaking landscapes, including Norway’s fjords, glaciers, lakes, and forests. Our journey will take us to Sognefjord, the largest fjord in Norway. Along the way, we’ll stop at the stunning Tvindefossen Waterfall. Then, we’ll embark on a magnificent cruise between Gudvangen and Aurland, enjoying the stunning scenery during this hour-and-a-half voyage. Continuing our adventure, we’ll traverse magnificent snow-covered landscapes and pass through the Aurland Tunnel, the longest in the world. We’ll visit the exterior of Borgund’s 12th-century wooden church, known as Norway’s best-preserved medieval church. Finally, we’ll arrive in Oslo with free time to explore the port, main streets, and the artwork in Frogner Park.

We include a panoramic tour of the Norwegian capital with a local guide. This city blends vibrant modern architecture with classical buildings, stunning scenery, and large parks with sculptures. Oslo has been named the European Green Capital. After the tour, you´ll have free time to explore on your own.

Next, we’ll depart for Sweden, passing through landscapes of forests and lakes. Our next stop is Örebro, a historic town by a beautiful lake, with a castle and a charming old town. Enjoy some time for a stroll before we continue to Stockholm, arriving in the evening.

Today we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of this beautiful capital city, built on 14 interconnected islands. We’ll visit Gamla Stan, also known as the “the city between bridges”, the lively old quarter filled with monumental buildings such as the Nobel Prize Museum, located right beside the main square of Stortorget which honors Nobel Prize winners, the Cathedral, and the Royal Palace. In the morning, we’ll enjoy a panoramic city tour with a local guide of the Danish capital, known for its exceptional quality of life, commitment to sustainability and rich cultural life. We’ll see The Little Mermaid, Nyhavn harbor with its colorful historic buildings, the exterior of Amalienborg Palace, home to the Danish royal family, the Town Hall Square, and the Tivoli Park area. After that we’ll leave for the south of Denmark. In Gedser we’ll take a two-hour ferry to cross to Germany. We’ll arrive in Warnemünde and continue towards Berlin, arriving at the end of the day.

Our visit will also include stops at the Holocaust Memorial and the Berlin Wall Memorial Museum. After that, enjoy some time in the afternoon.

packages.package_name: From Frankfurt to Copenhagen: A Pan-European Odyssey
packages.overview:
• Frankfurt – A dynamic blend of modern skyscrapers and historic charm welcomes you to this vibrant German metropolis.
  • Rhine River Cruise – Sail through fairy-tale landscapes, passing medieval castles and lush vineyards along the picturesque riverbanks.
  • Cochem Castle – A majestic fortress perched above the Moselle River, offering breathtaking views and medieval charm.
  • Cologne Cathedral – Marvel at this towering Gothic masterpiece, once the world’s tallest building, dominating the city skyline.
  • Fairy Tale Route – Wander through storybook villages, visiting Rapunzel’s Tower and the legendary Pied Piper’s Hamelin.
  • Hamburg – Discover Germany’s lively port city, with its striking architecture, bustling waterfront, and maritime heritage.
  • Stavanger – Stroll through this picturesque Norwegian town, renowned for its fjordside charm and Viking history.
  • Bergen & Fløyen Viewpoint – Ride the cable car for panoramic views of Norway’s stunning fjords and colorful harbor.
  • Sognefjord Cruise – Glide through Norway’s largest and most spectacular fjord, surrounded by majestic mountains and waterfalls.
  • Oslo & Frogner Park – Explore Norway’s stylish capital, home to grand palaces, modern architecture, and Vigeland’s iconic sculptures.
  • Stockholm & Gamla Stan – Discover Sweden’s enchanting capital, built on 14 islands, with cobbled streets and royal grandeur.
  • Helsingor & Kronborg Castle – Step into Shakespeare’s world at Hamlet’s legendary castle on the Danish coastline.
  • Copenhagen & Nyhavn – Experience Denmark’s capital, where colorful harbor houses and royal landmarks exude Nordic charm.

Take some time to relax or explore the surroundings at your own pace. In the afternoon, feel free to check the hotel reception boards for important details about the start of your exciting tour. 

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Frankfurt Airport","to":"Frankfurt","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":690,"h_name":"MARITIM FRANKFURT","h_country":"Germany","h_city":"Frankfurt","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Frankfurt/MARITIM FRANKFURT/1741155474506-2022-07-04.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Frankfurt/MARITIM FRANKFURT/1741155474588-2023-01-26.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Frankfurt/MARITIM FRANKFURT/1741155474629-unnamed (53).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Dortmund","country":"Germany","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Departure Frankfurt with Cruise on the Rhine & Arrival at Dortmund","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll explore the Rhine and Moselle Valleys, two rivers full of history and dotted with charming villages. We´ll begin with a short cruise on the Rhine, between the villages of Rüdesheim and Sankt Goar, undoubtedly the most picturesque section of the river. Next, we’ll head to the Moselle River, stopping for lunch in Cochem, a picturesque village on the Rhine riverbank overlooked by its imposing castle, which dates back over a thousand years. Afterward, we’ll travel to Cologne, where you´ll have time to visit its impressive Gothic cathedral. Standing at 154 meters tall, it was the tallest building in the world until the late 19th century. You can also enjoy a walk along the Rhine River and through the lively city center before we continue to our hotel in Dortmund.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Frankfurt","to":"Dortmund","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":691,"h_name":"MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM","h_country":"Germany","h_city":"Dortmund","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Dortmund/MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM/1741155695447-LogoEingangMercureDortmundCentrum - komprimiert.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Dortmund/MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM/1741155695481-2024-10-15.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Dortmund/MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM/1741155695551-2024-11-19.jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Hanover","country":"Germany","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Dortmund - Hanover","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll embark on a magical journey, reminiscing about our favorite childhood stories and movies as we travel through the towns that inspired the Brothers Grimm, now known as the Fairy Tale Route. Our first stop will be Trendelburg, a picturesque walled village where we’ll visit the large medieval castle and the famous Rapunzel Tower (admission included).



We’ll continue along scenic roads, crossing forests that will remind us of the tale of Hansel and Gretel. Our next stop will be Höxter, a vibrant town where we’ll enjoy lunch and visit Corvey Abbey, an ancient 9th-century monastery and World Heritage Site (admission included). We’ll then make a brief stop in Polle to photograph the exterior ruins of Cinderella’s Castle. Our journey will continue to Hamelin, renowned for the tale of “The Pied Piper of Hamelin”. After following the mouse tracks, we’ll proceed to Hanover, where we’ll have time to explore the city center and take a stroll next to the old town hall.

After breakfast, we’ll head to Hamburg, where we’ll see its lively port surrounding the lake. Following lunch, we’ll travel to Flensburg, a charming seaside village with a marina on the border with Denmark. Our journey will then continue along the Jutland peninsula until we reach Aarhus, Denmark’s second-largest city.

We’ll travel to Hirtshals and take a ferry across the North Sea to Norway, embarking on a three-hour voyage to Kristiansand. From there, we’ll continue along Norway’s stunning southern coastline. Our next stop will be the charming little town of Flekkefjord. We’ll then arrive in Stavanger, a picturesque town with a bustling port by the fjord.

Upon leaving the city, we’ll stop at the Swords in Rock monument, a tribute to the Viking era, before continuing our scenic route through tunnels, bridges, and some segments by boat along the fjord coast of Norway. One of these undersea tunnels is the deepest in the world. The two boat trips will make this part of the journey both relaxing and spectacular. We’ll arrive in Bergen at lunchtime and take a cable car up Mount Fløyen for amazing views of the town and the Bergen fjord. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore the capital city of fjords.

After a scenic journey through the heart of Norway, you’ll bid farewell to the charming city of Bergen, known for its colorful harbor and fjord-side beauty. As you depart, soak in the last glimpses of the mountains and coastal landscapes that define western Norway. The route to Oslo offers stunning views of lush valleys, deep fjords, and picturesque countryside. Upon arrival in Oslo, the vibrant capital welcomes you with its modern architecture, historic landmarks, and cultural richness. This transition marks the perfect blend of Norway’s natural wonders and urban elegance, offering a complete Nordic experience.

packages.package_name: Swiss Serenity & Italian Passione
packages.overview:
• Begin your European journey in Switzerland’s vibrant lakeside city.

  • Enjoy alpine adventures and explore the charming town of Lucerne.

  • Relax or explore Zurich at your own pace.

  • Travel through the scenic Alps into Italy’s fashion capital.

  • Discover the elegance and serenity of Lake Como.

  • Arrive in the enchanting city of canals.

  • Experience Venice’s magic from the water.

  • Travel to the Renaissance heart of Italy.

  • Explore Tuscany’s most iconic gems.

  • Head to the Eternal City filled with ancient wonders.

  • Walk through Rome’s legendary ruins.

  • Discover masterpieces of art and spirituality.

  • Say goodbye with memories of a lifetime.

Upon arrival at Zurich International Airport, you’ll be greeted and transferred to your hotel for check-in. Take some time to relax and recover from your journey. If time allows, head out to explore the city’s charming old town, stylish boutiques, or enjoy a peaceful stroll along Lake Zurich. From its medieval architecture to its modern urban flair, Zurich offers a perfect blend of culture, history, and natural beauty, making it an ideal starting point for your European adventure.

Embark on a breathtaking journey to Mount Titlis and the charming city of Lucerne, guided by our expert Travelxploria representative and the best on-ground support staff. Begin by exploring Lucerne's historic Old Town, Chapel Bridge, and the Lion Monument, immersing yourself in the city's rich history. Then, ascend into the heart of the Swiss Alps, where you'll ride the Rotair revolving cable car to 3,020 meters (9,908 feet). Once at the top, experience the Glacier Cave, Ice Flyer Chairlift, and Europe's highest suspension bridge, the Cliff Walk. Revel in snow-capped peaks, thrilling activities, and stunning alpine views on this unforgettable Swiss adventure, brought to you by Travelxploria, the best travel agency.

Enjoy a full day at leisure in Zurich, giving you the perfect opportunity to explore this sophisticated Swiss city at your own pace. Begin your day with a relaxed breakfast, then consider wandering through the charming Altstadt (Old Town), where cobbled streets, historic buildings, and boutique shops create a picturesque atmosphere. Stroll along Bahnhofstrasse, one of the world’s most exclusive shopping avenues, or relax by the serene shores of Lake Zurich. You may also choose to visit museums, such as the Swiss National Museum, or take a short hike up Uetliberg Mountain for panoramic city views. The day is yours to unwind and discover.

The journey takes approximately four hours, offering both relaxation and spectacular views. Upon arrival in Milan, Italy’s fashion and design capital, transfer to your hotel and check in. Spend the remainder of the day at leisure, soaking in the city's vibrant atmosphere, historic architecture, and stylish charm as you begin your Italian adventure.

Travel comfortably to this iconic destination, renowned for its stunning scenery, elegant villas, and charming lakeside towns. Explore the picturesque town of Como, stroll along its quaint streets, and visit the magnificent cathedral. Enjoy a scenic boat ride on the lake, passing through beautiful towns like Bellagio or Varenna. Capture postcard-perfect views of the lake and surrounding mountains. After a day immersed in natural beauty and serene elegance, return to Milan with unforgettable memories of this idyllic retreat.

Embark on an unforgettable Tuscan adventure with Travelxploria, your gateway to Italy’s most enchanting landscapes. Join our expert Travelxploria guide and best on-ground support staff as you explore the historic gems of Pisa, Siena, San Gimignano, and Chianti in a single breathtaking day. Capture the perfect photo at the Leaning Tower of Pisa, stroll through the medieval streets of Siena, and admire the grandeur of its famous Piazza del Campo. Lose yourself in the fairytale charm of San Gimignano, known for its ancient towers and artisan shops, before indulging in the rich flavors of Chianti’s world-renowned wines. From stunning architecture to rolling vineyard-covered hills, every moment is a feast for the senses. With Travelxploria, expect seamless travel, expert storytelling, and a truly immersive experience—because we don’t just plan tours, we craft unforgettable memories.

Travel from Florence to Rome aboard a high-speed train, enjoying a swift and scenic ride through the picturesque landscapes of Tuscany and Lazio. The journey takes approximately 1.5 hours, offering a comfortable and efficient way to transition from the artistic treasures of Florence to the timeless grandeur of Rome. Relax in your seat as you pass rolling hills, vineyards, and charming Italian towns. Upon arrival at Rome Termini Station, transfer to your hotel and settle in. Use the rest of the day to start exploring the Eternal City, rich with ancient ruins, Renaissance architecture, and vibrant local culture around every corner.

packages.package_name: Wandering Through Swiss Wonderland
packages.overview:
• Touch down in Zurich and settle into the heart of Switzerland.

  • Explore Zurich’s highlights, enjoy a lake cruise, and visit the Lindt Home of Chocolate.

  • Travel to the luxurious alpine resort town of St. Moritz.

  • Embark on a scenic ride aboard the world-famous Glacier Express.

  • Journey from the Matterhorn village to the lakeside city of Geneva.

  • Discover the breathtaking beauty of the French Alps on a full-day excursion.

  • Bid farewell to Switzerland with unforgettable alpine memories.

visit.","day_summary":"

Your Zurich adventure begins as you meet our friendly Travelxploria representative, ready to guide you through this stunning Swiss city. With the best on-ground support staff and an expert Travelxploria guide, immerse yourself in Zurich’s rich history, culture, and breathtaking scenery. Stroll along the famous Bahnhofstrasse, explore the charming Old Town, and soak in the tranquil beauty of Lake Zurich. Then, hop aboard a scenic boat cruise, gliding past the city’s picturesque skyline from a whole new perspective. The journey culminates at the Lindt Home of Chocolate, where the secrets of Swiss chocolate-making come to life. Indulge in irresistible tastings and witness the artistry behind Switzerland’s finest confections. With Travelxploria—the best travel agency—every detail is expertly handled, ensuring a seamless and unforgettable experience. From history to indulgence, this tour offers the perfect blend of culture, relaxation, and sweet delights.

The train ride offers a breathtaking introduction to the Swiss Alps, taking you through charming villages, lush valleys, and majestic mountain landscapes. As you ascend into the Engadin region, the air becomes crisper and the views more dramatic. Upon arrival in the luxurious alpine resort town of St. Moritz, check in to your hotel and enjoy the fresh mountain air. Spend the remainder of the day at leisure, exploring the elegant town known for its natural beauty and world-class charm.

Experience the breathtaking beauty of Zermatt and Mt. Gornergrat with your Travelxploria representative, ensuring the most unforgettable journey through the Swiss Alps. With the best on-ground support staff and an expert Travelxploria guide, begin your adventure with a scenic village walk through Zermatt, a charming, car-free town nestled at the base of the legendary Matterhorn. Wander past traditional wooden chalets, historic churches, and vibrant streets before boarding the iconic Gornergrat Railway, Europe’s highest open-air cogwheel train. As you ascend to 3,089 meters, be mesmerized by panoramic views of the Matterhorn and 29 other towering, snow-covered peaks. Capture stunning photographs, breathe in the crisp mountain air, and revel in the serenity of the Alps. With Travelxploria, the best travel agency, this once-in-a-lifetime experience blends nature, adventure, and Swiss charm into a journey you’ll cherish forever.

After breakfast in the picturesque mountain village of Zermatt, check out from your hotel and make your way to the train station for a scenic journey to Geneva. The train ride offers stunning views of the Swiss Alps, tranquil lakes, and charming countryside as you descend from the high-altitude serenity of Zermatt to the cosmopolitan elegance of Geneva. Relax in comfort as you travel through some of Switzerland’s most beautiful landscapes. Upon arrival, you’ll be transferred to your hotel. Take the rest of the day to unwind or begin exploring Geneva’s beautiful lakeside promenades, international flair, and historic Old Town.

packages.package_name: Best of Switzerland
packages.overview:
• Begin your Swiss journey in the vibrant and scenic city of Zurich.

  • Travel to the charming lakeside town of Lucerne by train.

  • Experience snow-covered peaks and glaciers at Mount Titlis.

  • Explore Mt. Pilatus via cableway and cogwheel train for alpine views.

  • Head to Interlaken, the adventure capital nestled between two lakes.

  • Enjoy a free day to relax or explore Interlaken at your own pace.

  • Travel to the car-free mountain village of Zermatt, home of the Matterhorn.

  • Journey to Geneva, Switzerland’s cosmopolitan lakeside city.

  • Discover Geneva’s highlights, from the Jet d’Eau to the UN headquarters.

  • Conclude your Swiss adventure and head home with lasting memories.

After breakfast, check out from your hotel in Zurich and head to the train station for a scenic journey to Lucerne. The comfortable train ride takes just under an hour, offering picturesque views of rolling hills, tranquil lakes, and charming Swiss villages. Upon arrival in Lucerne, make your way to the hotel and settle in. Spend the rest of the day exploring this postcard-perfect town nestled beside Lake Lucerne and surrounded by mountains.

Embark on a thrilling full-day Mount Titlis tour, a highlight of your Swiss adventure. From Lucerne, travel to Engelberg and ascend to Mount Titlis via a series of cable cars, including the famous rotating Rotair gondola. At the summit, marvel at the breathtaking panoramic views of the Alps and experience eternal snow and glacial landscapes. Explore the Glacier Cave, cross the thrilling Cliff Walk suspension bridge, and enjoy fun snow activities like tubing or snow sledding. This unforgettable alpine experience offers an incredible mix of adventure, natural beauty, and stunning snowy vistas that will leave you mesmerized.

Pilatus tour.","day_summary":"

Begin your adventure from Lucerne with a scenic cruise across Lake Lucerne to Alpnachstad, where you board the world’s steepest cogwheel railway to reach the summit of Mt. Pilatus. Enjoy spectacular panoramic views of the Swiss Alps and surrounding lakes from the top. Take in the fresh mountain air, explore hiking trails, or relax at one of the mountaintop restaurants. Later, descend via the aerial cableway and gondolas to Kriens, then return to Lucerne. This tour perfectly blends breathtaking landscapes, exciting transport experiences, and serene alpine beauty, making it a truly memorable Swiss mountain journey.

Depart from Lucerne and embark on a picturesque train journey to Interlaken aboard the scenic Luzern–Interlaken Express. This route is part of the famous GoldenPass Line, offering breathtaking views of sparkling lakes, lush valleys, charming villages, and majestic mountain peaks. The journey takes around two hours and is a visual delight from start to finish. Upon arrival in Interlaken, nestled between Lake Thun and Lake Brienz, check in to your hotel and relax. With the dramatic backdrop of the Jungfrau region, Interlaken welcomes you with its serene beauty and adventurous spirit, setting the tone for an exciting alpine experience.

Enjoy a leisurely day in Interlaken, the heart of the Bernese Oberland, surrounded by the towering peaks of the Eiger, Mönch, and Jungfrau. This day is yours to explore at your own pace—take a peaceful walk along Lake Thun or Lake Brienz, browse local shops for Swiss souvenirs, or relax in a cozy café with breathtaking alpine views. For those seeking adventure, optional activities like paragliding, a scenic Harder Kulm funicular ride, or a boat cruise on the lakes can elevate your day. Whether tranquil or thrilling, Interlaken offers the perfect balance of nature, charm, and unforgettable Swiss hospitality.

Depart Interlaken and journey to the stunning mountain village of Zermatt by train, a scenic ride that showcases some of Switzerland’s most breathtaking landscapes. The route typically involves a transfer at Visp, where you board the cogwheel train that winds its way up to car-free Zermatt. Marvel at the changing views—lush valleys, alpine meadows, and snow-capped peaks. Upon arrival, take in the crisp mountain air and the iconic Matterhorn towering in the background. Check in to your hotel and spend the rest of the day exploring this charming alpine village, known for its pristine beauty and world-class mountain experiences.

to Geneva By Train","day_summary":"

Leave the enchanting village of Zermatt and embark on a scenic train journey to Geneva, Switzerland’s cosmopolitan city by the lake. The trip begins with a descent on the cogwheel train to Visp, where you connect to a high-speed train that travels through the picturesque Rhône Valley. Enjoy views of vineyards, alpine landscapes, and charming towns along the way. As you approach Geneva, the scenery transitions to a vibrant urban skyline beside Lake Geneva. Upon arrival, check into your hotel and begin soaking in the city’s refined atmosphere, rich history, and international flair, with plenty of lakeside charm awaiting discovery.

packages.package_name: Classic Greek Odyssey
packages.overview:
• Athens – The Cradle of Civilization

  • Santorini – A Romantic Paradise

    • Explore the Acropolis of Athens, home to the Parthenon and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

    • Visit the Temple of Olympian Zeus and Ancient Agora, once the heart of Athenian democracy.

    • Wander through the charming Plaka District, lined with neoclassical houses, tavernas, and boutiques.

  • Mykonos – The Island of Glamour

    • Witness the world-famous sunset from Oia Village, with its blue-domed churches and whitewashed houses.

    • Relax at Red Beach and Perissa Beach, famous for their volcanic sands and crystal-clear waters.

    • Visit the ancient ruins of Akrotiri, a Minoan city preserved under volcanic ash.

  • Crete – The Land of Legends

    • Stroll through Mykonos Town (Chora) with its narrow alleyways, windmills, and vibrant nightlife.

    • Unwind on the pristine shores of Paradise Beach, known for its lively beach clubs.

    • Take a boat trip to Delos Island, one of Greece’s most important mythological and archaeological sites.

  1. Meteora – Monasteries in the Sky

    • Explore the Palace of Knossos, the center of the Minoan civilization.

    • Hike the Samaria Gorge, one of Europe’s longest and most scenic gorges.

    • Visit Elafonissi Beach, known for its pink sand and shallow turquoise waters.

  • Delphi – The Oracle’s Legacy

    • Discover the awe-inspiring Meteora Monasteries, perched on towering rock formations.

    • Experience the serenity of this UNESCO-listed site, a spiritual retreat for centuries.

  • Zakynthos – The Ionian Gem

    • Marvel at the stunning Navagio Beach (Shipwreck Beach), accessible only by boat.

    • Swim in the Blue Caves, famous for their vibrant blue reflections.

    • Visit the ancient sanctuary of Apollo at Delphi, once the most revered oracle of the ancient world.

    • Walk the Sacred Way and admire the ruins of the Temple of Apollo and Tholos of Delphi.

Welcome to your incredible journey with Travelxploria! Upon arrival, our friendly representative will greet you at the airport and ensure a smooth transfer to your hotel. Take this time to relax and soak in the first impressions of your destination. Whether you choose to unwind in your room, explore nearby attractions, or enjoy a leisurely stroll, the day is yours. Later in the afternoon, our representative will provide all the essential details about the upcoming adventure—either in person or through the information boards at the hotel reception.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Athens International Airport","to":"Athens","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":797,"h_name":"Novotel Athenes","h_country":"Greece","h_city":"Athens","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Athens/Novotel Athenes/1741591952144-2023-10-28.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Athens/Novotel Athenes/1741591952290-unnamed - 2025-03-10T130155.862.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Athens/Novotel Athenes/1741591952296-unnamed - 2025-03-10T130152.329.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"City Tour of Athens","day_summary":"

We´ll embark on a city tour of Athens with a local guide, visiting landmarks such as the Presidential Mansion, the Panathenaic Stadium where the first modern Olympic Games were held, the National Library, and the bustling squares of the city center. We´ll also explore the newer neighborhoods that developed after the 2004 Olympic Games. The tour will conclude at the Acropolis (admission not included). In the evening, we´ll provide a transfer to the historical neighborhood of Plaka, renowned for its charming Greek tavernas and opportunities to immerse yourself in the rich folklore of this country.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Kalabaka","country":"Greece","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Athens to Kalabaka","day_summary":"

We’ll depart early in the morning towards northern Greece, stopping at Kamena Vourla, a charming seaside tourist hub surrounded by stunning landscapes. We´ll then proceed north, stopping at the Thermopylae Mountain Pass, the historic site of the famous battle. Next, we´ll visit Kastraki, a picturesque village with beautiful views of the entire valley, where you´ll have free time for lunch. Afterward, we´ll explore the Meteora, a breathtaking UNESCO World Heritage Site. Admission to one of its renowned monasteries is included. Dinner will be provided in Kalabaka.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Athens","to":"Kalabaka","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":778,"h_name":"Divani Meteora Hotel","h_country":"Greece","h_city":"Kalabaka","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Kalabaka/Divani Meteora Hotel/1744887790593-Divani Meteora Hotel 2.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Kalabaka/Divani Meteora Hotel/1744887790726-Divani Meteora Hotel.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Kalabaka/Divani Meteora Hotel/1744887790765-Divani Meteora Hotel 1.jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Athens","country":"Greece","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Greece/Athens/1740987761004-istockphoto-1270775001-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Greece/Athens/1740987761066-istockphoto-157180942-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Greece/Athens/1740987761102-istockphoto-1028749698-612x612.jpg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Kalabaka to Athens","day_summary":"

We’ll journey south through the Thessaly region, where you’ll experience stunning landscapes, before reaching Delphi. Here, we´ll explore the exquisite archaeological site overlooking the Gulf of Corinth, with breathtaking views. There will be time for lunch. Following that, we´ll head to the Hosios Loukas Monastery, renowned as one of the most beautiful Byzantine monasteries in Greece and a World Heritage Site. Its magnificent 11th-century mosaics are truly remarkable. We´ll then continue our journey towards Athens, arriving in the evening.

You´ll have free time in the morning to enjoy the city, visit museums, or shop. In the afternoon, we´ll transfer to the Piraeus Port to board a night ferry, featuring cruise-like amenities. You´ll stay in a double cabin and spend the night on board.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Crete (Heraklion)","country":"Greece","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Greece/Crete (Heraklion)/1740987660038-istockphoto-1389134802-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Greece/Crete (Heraklion)/1740987660117-istockphoto-1215376391-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Greece/Crete (Heraklion)/1740987660157-istockphoto-1161120260-612x612.jpg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Heraklion Crete Tour","day_summary":"

Arrival at 7:00 a.m. in Heraklion on the island of Crete. We’ll travel along the coast towards the west of the island. Our first stop is Rethymno, a beautiful coastal city of Venetian origin, dominated by its medieval castle. Next, we’ll visit Chania, a captivating city with an impressive fortress and stately mansions. Chania has been contested by Romans, Byzantines, Venetians, Genoese, Turks, Egyptians, and Greeks throughout its history. We´ll return to Heraklion at the end of the day.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"Athens","to":"Crete (Heraklion)","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":851,"h_name":"Viking Star","h_country":"Greece","h_city":"Crete (Heraklion)","h_type":"Cruise","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Crete (Heraklion)/Viking Star/1744888164230-Viking.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Crete (Heraklion)/Viking Star/1744888164386-Viking 1.jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Heraklion","country":"Greece","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Historic & Cultural Excursion of Heraklion","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll embark on an excursion to the Palace of Knossos, where we´ll delve into the Minoan culture that laid the foundation for Greek civilization four thousand years ago (admission and guided tour included). After our visit, we’ll travel through the island´s tall mountains and journey southward to the shores of the Libyan Sea, arriving at Agia Galini—a picturesque town with winding streets, a charming fishing port, and crowded taverns. You’ll have time for lunch and, if you wish, a swim in the sea. We´ll return to the capital of Crete at day’s end.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Crete (Heraklion)","to":"Heraklion","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":801,"h_name":"ibis Styles Heraklion Central","h_country":"Greece","h_city":"Heraklion","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Heraklion/ibis Styles Heraklion Central/1741592385850-unnamed - 2025-03-10T130931.797.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Heraklion/ibis Styles Heraklion Central/1741592385919-unnamed - 2025-03-10T130921.145.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Heraklion/ibis Styles Heraklion Central/1741592385951-unnamed - 2025-03-10T130914.253.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Coastal Tour of Heraklion","day_summary":"

We’ll travel along the coast towards the east of the island. Our first stop will be in the small town of Kritsa to visit Panagia Kera, a 13th-century church renowned for its exquisite frescoes (admission included). Afterward, we’ll arrive at Agios Nikolaos, a picturesque town with a natural port. You’ll have time to stroll and enjoy lunch. Next, we’ll continue to Elounda, a former city-state turned into a beautiful tourist enclave. Here, we’ll embark on a boat ride to Spinalonga Island to visit the Fortress of Spinalonga, an ancient Venetian fortress later used as a leper colony for many years. After the visit, we’ll return by boat and head back to Heraklion for accommodation.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Athens","country":"Greece","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Greece/Athens/1740987761004-istockphoto-1270775001-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Greece/Athens/1740987761066-istockphoto-157180942-612x612.jpg"},{"path":"/Destination/Greece/Athens/1740987761102-istockphoto-1028749698-612x612.jpg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Heraklion to Athens","day_summary":"

Early in the morning, we’ll transfer to the port and board a high-speed ferry to Santorini, arriving between 09:00 and 10:00 a.m. From there, we’ll journey by bus through stunning landscapes to Oia, undoubtedly the most spectacular town on the island: vibrant white streets, beautiful cliff views, remnants of a castle, and windmills. You’ll have time for lunch and a leisurely stroll. Later, we’ll visit Fira, the island’s capital. Those interested can descend to the port by cable car or donkey and admire the cruise ships. Afterwards, we’ll proceed to the port to board a high-speed ferry departing around 03:15 p.m. Arrival in Athens expected around midnight.

packages.package_name: Greece to Italy Timeless Cities and Seaside Wonders
packages.overview:
• Athens

    • Acropolis of Athens: Home to the iconic Parthenon, a symbol of Ancient Greece.

    • Temple of Olympian Zeus: One of the largest temples of ancient Greece, located in the heart of Athens.

    • Plaka District: A charming neighborhood filled with traditional Greek houses, cafes, and shops.

    • National Archaeological Museum: Displays some of the most important ancient artifacts of Greek civilization.

  • Santorini

    • Oia Village: Famous for its whitewashed buildings, blue domes, and stunning sunsets over the Aegean Sea.

    • Fira Town: The island's bustling capital with shops, restaurants, and cliffside views.

    • Red Beach: A dramatic beach with red sand and crystal-clear waters.

    • Akrotiri Archaeological Site: An ancient Minoan settlement destroyed by a volcanic eruption.

  • Mykonos

    • Mykonos Town (Chora): Known for its narrow streets, whitewashed buildings, and vibrant nightlife.

    • Paradise Beach: A lively beach known for its beach clubs, bars, and parties.

    • Delos Island: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, known for its ancient ruins and historical significance.

    • Little Venice: A picturesque area in Mykonos Town with buildings right on the water.

  • Crete

    • Knossos Palace: The largest archaeological site of Minoan civilization.

    • Elafonissi Beach: Famous for its pink sand and clear waters.

    • Samaria Gorge: A popular hiking destination in the White Mountains, offering stunning views and natural beauty.

    • Heraklion Archaeological Museum: A museum showcasing Minoan artifacts from Crete.

Italy

  • Rome

    • Colosseum: The ancient amphitheater where gladiators once fought, one of the greatest symbols of Ancient Rome.

    • Vatican City: The smallest country in the world, home to the Pope and the famous St. Peter's Basilica and Sistine Chapel.

    • Roman Forum: The ruins of ancient government buildings and temples at the heart of ancient Rome.

    • Pantheon: A well-preserved ancient Roman temple with a stunning dome.

    • Trevi Fountain: A baroque masterpiece and one of the largest fountains in Rome.

  • Florence

    • Duomo di Firenze: The iconic cathedral with a stunning dome designed by Brunelleschi.

    • Uffizi Gallery: Home to world-famous artworks, including pieces by Botticelli, Michelangelo, and Leonardo da Vinci.

    • Ponte Vecchio: A historic bridge lined with shops selling jewelry, art, and souvenirs.

    • Piazza della Signoria: A lively square with Renaissance sculptures, including a replica of Michelangelo’s David.

  • Venice

    • St. Mark's Basilica: A stunning example of Byzantine architecture with golden mosaics.

    • Rialto Bridge: A famous bridge spanning the Grand Canal, lined with shops.

    • Gondola Ride: A romantic boat ride through the canals of Venice.

    • Doge’s Palace: A gothic-style palace once home to the Venetian rulers.

  • Milan

    • Duomo di Milano: A gothic cathedral, one of the largest in the world.

    • The Last Supper: Leonardo da Vinci’s famous painting housed in the Convent of Santa Maria delle Grazie.

    • Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II: An elegant shopping gallery, home to luxury stores and restaurants.

    • Sforza Castle: A historic fortress with museums and art collections.

  • Amalfi Coast

    • Positano: A picturesque cliffside town with colorful buildings and stunning beaches.

    • Ravello: Famous for its beautiful villas, including Villa Cimbrone and Villa Rufolo.

    • Amalfi Town: Known for its historical cathedral and lovely coastal scenery.

    • Capri Island: A glamorous island with upscale shopping, beaches, and the Blue Grotto.

Experience the best of ancient and modern Athens on this comprehensive guided tour with Travelxploria's expert guides. Begin at Syntagma Square, the heart of the city, where history and modern life blend seamlessly. Visit the Temple of Olympian Zeus, one of the largest temples of the ancient world, and marvel at its towering columns. Explore the National Archaeological Museum, home to an extensive collection of Greek artifacts, sculptures, and treasures from antiquity. With Travelxploria’s knowledgeable experts, gain deep insights into Athens’ rich heritage, architectural wonders, and vibrant culture, making this tour a perfect introduction to the city’s timeless charm.

We’ll travel to the Peloponnese, making a brief stop at the Corinth Canal, an impressive engineering marvel from the late nineteenth century. Continuing through the mountains, we’ll reach Tripoli, the capital of the Arcadia region. Here, we’ll take a break to explore the bustling Aeros Square, known for its cafes and restaurants. Our journey will then take us to Olympia, where we’ll visit the Archaeological Site with a local guide (tickets included). The first Olympic Games were held here in 776 B.C., attracting athletes from all over Greece and its colonies. Highlights of the site include the Temple of Zeus, the Stadium, the Gymnasium, and the Arena. Next, we’ll proceed to Patras, the third largest city in Greece, with a remarkable medieval fortress and the largest cathedral in the country. Adjacent to the cathedral is the small church of St. Andrew, a significant place of pilgrimage. Dinner will be included.

After breakfast, we’ll cross the Rio–Antirrio Bridge, a spectacular suspension bridge that connects the Peloponnese with the Greek mainland. We’ll travel through the regions of Central Greece and Epirus. Upon arrival in Igoumenitsa, a port town, there will be time for a stroll before boarding the modern ferry at 02:00 p.m. to sail across the Ionian Sea to Italy. We’ll reach Brindisi by the end of the day, around 09:00 p.m., where we’ll spend the night.

Today, we’ll visit two extraordinary and authentic towns. Our first stop will be Alberobello, famous for its unique conical-shaped houses known as trulli. These distinctive structures, with their stacked stone roofs, are a UNESCO World Heritage Site and represent a remarkable example of Italian rural architecture. Next, we’ll head to Matera, located in the Basilicata region, renowned for its ancient historic center and the Sassi di Matera—a complex of ancient dwellings and buildings carved into the rock, dating back thousands of years. Not only is it a World Heritage Site, but also one of the oldest continuously inhabited human settlements in the world. In the afternoon, after enjoying an included lunch in Matera, we’ll continue our journey through Basilicata and Campania, taking in the beautiful mountain landscapes. We’ll arrive in Naples by evening, where you’ll have free time to explore.

We’ll arrive in Paola, located on the shores of the Tyrrhenian Sea, where we’ll visit the Sanctuary of St. Francis of Paola and enjoy free time for lunch in its charming historic center. Finally, we’ll continue to Taormina. Dinner will be included.

Nestled at the foot of Mount Etna, the beautiful city of Taormina was once described by Churchill as the most beautiful place on earth. You’ll have time to explore the vibrant old town, where the Greek theater is set against the breathtaking landscape. Afterward, we’ll continue our journey toward the center of the island. Our next stop will be Caltagirone, a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its impressive Baroque architecture. You’ll get to ride a little tourist train (included), where you’ll learn about the city and its key landmarks. Caltagirone is also the ceramic capital of Sicily, as seen in its numerous shops, workshops, and iconic sites, including the famous 142-step staircase, each step uniquely decorated with colorful tiles. After lunch, you’ll tour Villa Romana del Casale with a local guide (admission and tour included), another UNESCO World Heritage Site. In the Roman Empire, villas were symbols of agricultural wealth, and Villa del Casale is exceptional not only for its well-preserved 4th-century architecture but also for its stunning mosaics, which adorn almost every room, considered the finest in the Roman world. We’ll then cross the island’s mountainous interior and continue to Palermo, arriving by the end of the day. Dinner will be included.

packages.package_name: Best of Balkan Region
packages.overview:
• Croatia: Home to beautiful beaches along the Adriatic coast, historic cities like Dubrovnik and Split, and the stunning Plitvice Lakes National Park. Croatia blends medieval architecture with spectacular coastal views and a rich cultural heritage.

  • Serbia: A blend of traditional and modern, Serbia offers historical landmarks such as Belgrade’s Kalemegdan Fortress, vibrant nightlife, and beautiful landscapes like Tara National Park. The country's culinary delights and lively festivals make it a unique destination.

  • Bosnia & Herzegovina: Sarajevo is known for its Ottoman, Austro-Hungarian, and Yugoslav history, with sites like the Sarajevo Tunnel and Baščaršija market. The picturesque town of Mostar is famous for the iconic Stari Most (Old Bridge) spanning the Neretva River.

  • Montenegro: A hidden gem with a coastline dotted with charming towns like Kotor and Budva, stunning beaches, and mountains. Montenegro is ideal for lovers of outdoor adventures, offering hiking, boating, and breathtaking views along the Bay of Kotor.

  • Albania: Known for its untouched nature, pristine beaches, and ancient ruins, Albania offers a more off-the-beaten-path experience. Explore UNESCO World Heritage sites like Gjirokastër, visit the beaches of the Albanian Riviera, and discover the charming city of Berat.

  • Kosovo: A relatively new country, Kosovo boasts a unique blend of history, culture, and hospitality. Visit the UNESCO-listed Decani Monastery, the vibrant capital of Pristina, and the tranquil town of Peja surrounded by the Rugova Mountains.

  • North Macedonia: Home to picturesque Lake Ohrid, North Macedonia offers historical gems like the ancient city of Ohrid, a UNESCO World Heritage site, and a blend of Slavic, Ottoman, and Byzantine influences in its architecture and culture.

  • Bulgaria: Known for its rich history, Bulgaria offers diverse landscapes from Black Sea beaches to mountain ranges. Explore Sofia’s historical sites, the ancient rock-hewn churches of Ivanovo, and the UNESCO-listed Rila Monastery.

  • Greece: Though often considered part of the Mediterranean, Greece’s northern regions, such as Thessaloniki and Meteora, showcase the diverse heritage and stunning landscapes of the Balkans. Visit ancient ruins, picturesque villages, and vibrant cities.

We’ll depart early in the morning to travel to Bosnia. Border formalities may take some time. As we drive through certain areas, we’ll be reminded of the tragic war of the 1990s. Our journey will take us through the majority-Serb territory of Bosnia, with our first stop in Banja Luka. There will be time for lunch. We’ll then continue to Jajce, arguably the have the opportunity to stroll through the historic center and visit the charming Bazaar, an old Ottoman market renowned for its architecture, oriental ambiance, and traditional shops. Dinner will be included.most beautiful town in Bosnia, celebrated for its cultural diversity, mosque, fortress, and impressive waterfall (admission included). After some time to explore, we’ll proceed to Sarajevo, arriving in the evening.

In the morning, we’ll enjoy a guided sightseeing tour in Sarajevo, a captivating city known as the ¨Jerusalem of Europe¨ due to its coexistence of several religions. Mosques, churches, and synagogues showcase its rich religious diversity. We’ll explore the old town´s strong Ottoman influence, highlighted by a magnificent mosque over 500 years old and a beautiful fountain. After our visit, we’ll continue through an area of stunning beauty to Mostar, with its enchanting historic center and iconic stone bridge that separates the Orthodox, Muslim, and Catholic communities. You’ll have time for lunch and a stroll through the city. We’ll then proceed to Croatia, where border procedures may take a while. We’ll arrive in Dubrovnik at the end of the day. Dinner will be included.

We’ll enjoy a panoramic tour of Dubrovnik with a local guide in the morning. We will undoubtedly be in one of the most beautiful cities in Europe, perched on a cliff, surrounded by walls and kissed by the Adriatic Sea. Its intact historic center is a jewel of world architectural heritage. We’ll have the opportunity to enjoy a stunning view of the city from a scenic viewpoint along the road. The tour includes tickets to the Franciscan Monastery, home to the oldest pharmacy in Europe, and the Assumption Cathedral. We’ll also take a boat trip to Lokrum, a small island just 15 minutes away that is home to the ruins of an eleventh-century Benedictine monastery, a popular setting for the series Game of Thrones. Afterward, you’ll have free time to explore at your leisure before returning to the hotel in the late afternoon. Dinner will be included.

Next, we’ll stop in Budva, a charming walled village surrounded by the sea, for lunch. Then we’ll travel to Albania. Around midday, we’ll head inland, passing through Albania’s tall mountain scenery and little villages. Enjoy the beautiful countryside around Lake Ohrid, which forms the border with Macedonia. We’ll continue to the Republic of Macedonia (border procedures) and stop for lunch in Struga, a picturesque town on the lake´s shores. We’ll then proceed to Ohrid, a city rich in Ottoman influence and a designated World Heritage Site. You’ll have time to explore the Plaosnik Monastery, or the ancient university founded in the ninth century. Dinner will be included.

We’ll journey through Macedonia into Greece, with a stop in Bitola, a city rich in history and charm, known for its Ottoman and neoclassical architecture. You’ll have time to stroll along Širok Sokak, a lively area filled with cafes, restaurants, and shops. After completing border formalities, we’ll enter Greece, where the northern region is deeply influenced by Slavic and Ottoman culture and features rugged landscapes. Our journey will continue to the Meteora region. Just outside Kalabaka, we’ll stop in Kastraki, a picturesque village offering stunning views of the valley, where you’ll have time for lunch before going to the Meteora Valley, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. We’ll drive through the entire valley and include admission to one of the most famous cliffside monasteries. Arrival in Kalabaka, with dinner included.

Our first stop will be in Thessaloniki, where we’ll have time to explore and enjoy lunch in Greece’s second-largest city. Afterward, we’ll continue into Bulgaria, crossing the border into the region of Bulgarian Macedonia. Here, we’ll visit Melnik, a picturesque museum town nestled among stunning landscapes and renowned for its wine production. We’ll conclude our day in Sandanski, a charming spa town known as the birthplace of Spartacus where we’ll stay for the night. Dinner will be included.

With its three churches, this monastery offers a serene and peaceful atmosphere. We’ll then proceed to Plovdiv, where lunch is included before going on a tour of this city, one of the oldest in Europe. Our tour includes admission to the Roman Amphitheatre, the Old Pharmacy, and the Armenian House.

Today’s journey will take us through the stunning landscapes of the Balkan Mountains. In the Rose Valley, we’ll visit Kazanlak, where admission is included to see the replica of the Thracian Tomb of Kazanlak, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. We’ll also learn about the local production of rose-based products. Amidst the mountains, we’ll stop at the Shipka Memorial Church, a memorial to the Russo-Turkish War. We’ll then explore Etar, a charming village and ethnographic museum. After lunch (included), we’ll travel to Arbanasi, an ancient trading city known for its fortress-like houses, where we’ll visit the Nativity Church (admission included). Our day will end in Veliko Tarnovo, Bulgaria’s former capital, renowned for its picturesque medieval center beside its canyon.

packages.package_name: Hidden Gems of Bulgaria and the Balkans
packages.overview:
Bulgaria – The Jewel of the Balkans

  • Sofia: The capital city blending Roman ruins, Orthodox churches, and modern life.

  • Rila Monastery: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and Bulgaria’s most famous monastery.

  • Plovdiv: One of Europe’s oldest cities, home to a well-preserved Roman amphitheater and charming Old Town.

  • Veliko Tarnovo: The medieval capital of Bulgaria, featuring Tsarevets Fortress.

  • Black Sea Coast: Golden Sands & Sunny Beach offer stunning beaches and vibrant nightlife.

  • Bansko & Pirin Mountains: A top ski and hiking destination with breathtaking landscapes.

  • Bulgarian Cuisine: Try banitsa (cheese pastry), shopska salad, and rakia (fruit brandy).

The Balkans – A Mosaic of Cultures & Landscapes

  • Serbia: Explore Belgrade’s historic Kalemegdan Fortress and lively nightlife.

  • Romania: Visit Bucharest’s Palace of Parliament and Transylvania’s Dracula Castle.

  • North Macedonia: Discover the stunning Ohrid Lake and the capital, Skopje.

  • Montenegro: Enjoy the scenic Bay of Kotor and the picturesque beaches of Budva.

  • Albania: Experience Tirana’s vibrant streets and the pristine beaches of the Albanian Riviera.
