As the day winds down, enjoy a delicious included dinner, while our best-on-ground support staff ensures everything runs smoothly. Embark on a poignant journey through Bosnia with Travelxploria, where every moment is guided with care and insight by our experienced tour guide and dedicated support team. After an early morning departure, we’ll cross the border and pass through regions shaped by the scars of the 1990s war. Our first stop is Banja Luka, where you’ll have time to explore the capital of Republika Srpska, famous for its fortress and Orthodox cathedral. After lunch, we’ll continue to Jajce, a town celebrated for its cultural blend, magnificent waterfall, and historical sites (admission included). Here, you’ll have time to explore and soak in the town’s charm. Finally, we’ll head to Sarajevo, where our Travelxploria representative ensures seamless transfers and logistics, allowing you to relax and enjoy the sights, including the historic Bazaar. Dinner will be included, with your evening filled with unforgettable experiences. Let Travelxploria make your journey through Bosnia enriching and hassle-free.

Join Travelxploria for an unforgettable day, beginning with a guided sightseeing tour of Sarajevo, known as the \"Jerusalem of Europe\" for its rich religious diversity. Explore the old town’s Ottoman influence, including a 500-year-old mosque and a stunning fountain. Our experienced Travelxploria guide will lead you through this captivating city, where mosques, churches, and synagogues coexist harmoniously. Afterward, we’ll head to Mostar, a city of breathtaking beauty, where the iconic stone bridge divides Orthodox, Muslim, and Catholic communities. You’ll have time for lunch and a leisurely stroll through this enchanting historic center. As we continue to Croatia, our dedicated Travelxploria representative ensures a smooth transition through border procedures, allowing you to relax and enjoy the scenery. We’ll arrive in Dubrovnik in the evening, where dinner will be included, and you’ll have the chance to unwind after a day of exploration. With Travelxploria, enjoy seamless pick-ups, drop-offs, and exceptional support throughout your journey.

Discover the beauty of Dubrovnik with Travelxploria on a panoramic tour of one of Europe's most stunning cities. Perched on a cliff and surrounded by ancient walls, Dubrovnik is a true architectural gem, and our expert local guide will bring its history to life. Enjoy breathtaking views from a scenic viewpoint and explore the Franciscan Monastery, home to Europe's oldest pharmacy, and the magnificent Assumption Cathedral. Our journey continues with a boat trip to Lokrum Island, where you'll visit the ruins of an eleventh-century Benedictine monastery, a popular filming location for Game of Thrones. After the tour, you’ll have free time to explore the city’s charming streets at your leisure. Throughout your day, our Travelxploria representative and on-the-ground support team will ensure everything runs smoothly, from seamless transfers to providing assistance whenever you need it. In the evening, we’ll return to the hotel for a relaxing dinner. Let Travelxploria make your Dubrovnik experience truly unforgettable.

Embark on a scenic adventure with Travelxploria as we journey from Montenegro to Albania, guided by our expert tour guide and supported by our dedicated on-the-ground team. After an early start and border procedures, we’ll marvel at the stunning coastal views of the Bay of Kotor. A ferry ride will take us across the bay to Kotor, a charming walled town influenced by Venetian architecture, where you’ll have time to explore. Next, we’ll head to Budva, a picturesque walled village by the sea, for a leisurely lunch. Dinner will be included to round off the day.

Today, embark on an incredible journey with Travelxploria, where history and natural beauty come together seamlessly. We’ll begin by traveling through Macedonia, passing the historic city of Bitola, known as the \"City of Consuls.\" After crossing the border into Greece, our expert tour guide will lead you through the northern region, rich in Slavic and Ottoman influences, with stunning rugged landscapes. We’ll stop in Kastoria, famous for its 100 churches and medieval streets, where you’ll have time to enjoy lunch by the lakeside. In the afternoon, we’ll continue to Kalabaka, home to the awe-inspiring Meteora rock formations. Here, we’ll visit one of its famous monasteries (admission included), perched high on the cliffs, offering both spiritual history and breathtaking views. Throughout the day, our Travelxploria representative and on-the-ground support staff will ensure smooth pick-ups, drop-offs, and personalized care, allowing you to fully immerse yourself in the experience. Dinner will be included, capping off a remarkable day.

Our day begins with stunning mountain views as we head towards Thessaloniki, Greece’s vibrant second-largest city. Here, you’ll have free time to explore and enjoy a delicious lunch. Our day concludes in Sandanski, a charming spa town known as the birthplace of Spartacus, where you’ll unwind and enjoy a restful stay. Bidding farewell to the Balkans marks the end of a mesmerizing journey through a region rich in cultural diversity, breathtaking landscapes, and deep-rooted history, where ancient fortresses and vibrant old towns tell tales of empires past, scenic coastlines along the Adriatic and rugged mountain ranges leave lasting impressions, and the warmth of local hospitality lingers just as fondly as the taste of traditional dishes and regional wines, as you reflect on moments spent exploring charming cities like Dubrovnik, Kotor, Sarajevo, and Belgrade, cruising through lakes and valleys, wandering cobbled streets and bustling markets, and embracing a tapestry of languages, traditions, and unforgettable encounters—this bittersweet goodbye is more than just the end of a trip; it’s a heartfelt departure from a land that stirs the soul and invites you to return, carrying with you memories woven from history, nature, and the spirit of the Balkan people.

packages.package_name: Scandinavian Delight
packages.overview:
• Copenhagen, Denmark – Explore the colorful Nyhavn waterfront, visit the Little Mermaid statue, stroll through Tivoli Gardens, and admire the Rosenborg Castle.
  • Stockholm, Sweden – Discover the beauty of the Swedish capital, with attractions like Gamla Stan (Old Town), the Royal Palace, the Vasa Museum, and enjoy a scenic cruise through the Stockholm Archipelago.
  • Helsinki, Finland – Experience the blend of modern design and historic charm, visiting sites such as Helsinki Cathedral, Suomenlinna Fortress, and the vibrant Market Square.
  • Tallinn, Estonia – Step back in time in Tallinn’s medieval Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, featuring cobblestone streets, gothic churches, and fairy-tale architecture.

Welcome to your unforgettable journey with Travelxploria! As soon as you arrive, our dedicated Travelxploria representative will be there to greet you with a warm welcome and ensure a smooth transfer to your hotel. Take some time to relax and settle in, or explore your surroundings at your own pace. In the afternoon, our representative will be available to provide you with all the details you need for the adventure ahead. You can also find important tour information displayed on the boards at the hotel reception.

In the morning, we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of the Danish capital with a local guide. Copenhagen, the largest Scandinavian city, is full of contrasts, featuring the popular Tivoli Gardens, Christiansborg Palace, the charming terraces of Nyhavn, and the iconic Little Mermaid statue. In the late morning, we’ll journey to Jutland. Our route includes crossing a 20 km bridge over the sea and stopping at Odense, where we can explore its Gothic cathedral and H.C. Andersen’s House. Upon arrival in Aarhus, you’ll have free time.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Copenhagen","to":"AARHUS","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":695,"h_name":"Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham","h_country":"Denmark","h_city":"AARHUS","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562158-unnamed (59).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562232-2021-04-14.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562273-unnamed (58).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Stavanger","country":"Norway","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Aarhus to Kristiansand by Kristiansand Ferry & Stay at Stavanger","day_summary":"

We’ll travel to Hirtshals and take a ferry across the North Sea to Norway, embarking on a three-hour voyage to Kristiansand. From there, we’ll continue along Norway’s stunning southern coastline. Our next stop will be the charming little town of Flekkefjord. We’ll then arrive in Stavanger, a picturesque town with a bustling port by the fjord.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"AARHUS","to":"Stavanger","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":697,"h_name":"Clarion Hotel Energy","h_country":"Norway","h_city":"Stavanger","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Stavanger/Clarion Hotel Energy/1741157572875-unnamed (63).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Stavanger/Clarion Hotel Energy/1741157572952-Cl_Energy_RM_13_standard_enkel.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Stavanger/Clarion Hotel Energy/1741157572991-unnamed (62).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Bergen","country":"Norway","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Bergen/1742150469947-AdobeStock_187978352-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Bergen/1742150472633-AdobeStock_189721277-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Bergen/1742150473321-AdobeStock_297919201-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Stavanger to Bergen","day_summary":"

Upon leaving the city, we’ll stop at the Swords in Rock monument, a tribute to the Viking era, before continuing our scenic route through tunnels, bridges, and some segments by boat along the fjord coast of Norway. One of these undersea tunnels is the deepest in the world. The two boat trips will make this part of the journey both relaxing and spectacular. We’ll arrive in Bergen at lunchtime and take a cable car up Mount Fløyen for amazing views of the town and the Bergen fjord. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore the capital city of fjords.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Stavanger","to":"Bergen","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":699,"h_name":"Scandic Neptun","h_country":"Norway","h_city":"Bergen","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Bergen/Scandic Neptun/1741157889308-2021-10-16.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Bergen/Scandic Neptun/1741157889416-unnamed (67).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Bergen/Scandic Neptun/1741157889425-unnamed.png\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Oslo","country":"Norway","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149250639-AdobeStock_67424562-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149251103-AdobeStock_101002987-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149251252-AdobeStock_120502702-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149251569-AdobeStock_329111106-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Bergen to Sognefjord & Stay at Oslo","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll experience more breathtaking landscapes, including Norway’s fjords, glaciers, lakes, and forests. Our journey will take us to Sognefjord, the largest fjord in Norway. Along the way, we’ll stop at the stunning Tvindefossen Waterfall. Then, we’ll embark on a magnificent cruise between Gudvangen and Aurland, enjoying the stunning scenery during this hour-and-a-half voyage. We suggest having lunch on baord. Continuing our adventure, we’ll traverse magnificent snow-covered landscapes and pass through the Aurland Tunnel, the longest in the world. We’ll visit Borgund’s 12th-century wooden church, known as Norway’s best-preserved medieval church (admission included). Finally, we’ll arrive in Oslo with free time to explore the port, main streets, and the artwork in Frogner Park.

We include a panoramic tour of the Norwegian capital with a local guide. This city blends vibrant modern architecture with classical buildings, stunning scenery, and large parks with sculptures. Oslo has been named the European Green Capital. After the tour, you´ll have free time to explore on your own.

Next, we’ll depart for Sweden, passing through landscapes of forests and lakes. Our next stop is Örebro, a historic town by a beautiful lake, with a castle and a charming old town. Enjoy some time for a stroll before we continue to Stockholm, arriving in the evening.

packages.package_name: Baltic Delight Capitals Coastlines and Culture
packages.overview:
Tallinn, Estonia – A Fairytale Capital

  • Wander through Tallinn’s Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage site with cobblestone streets and medieval towers.

  • Visit Toompea Castle and enjoy panoramic views from the Kohtuotsa Viewing Platform.

  • Explore the Alexander Nevsky Cathedral, an architectural gem with Russian Orthodox influences.

  • Stroll along the lively Town Hall Square, lined with charming cafes and shops.

2. Riga, Latvia – Art Nouveau & Culture

  • Marvel at the stunning Art Nouveau architecture in the city center.

  • Discover the historic Riga Old Town, home to the House of the Blackheads and St. Peter’s Church.

  • Visit the bustling Central Market, one of Europe’s largest food markets.

  • Walk along the Daugava River Promenade for scenic views and relaxation.

3. Vilnius, Lithuania – A Blend of Cultures

  • Explore the Gothic and Baroque architecture in Vilnius Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

  • Visit the Gediminas Tower, offering breathtaking views of the city.

  • Discover the Uzupis District, a bohemian enclave known for its artistic vibe.

  • Experience the spiritual atmosphere at the Gates of Dawn, a historic religious site.

4. Helsinki, Finland – The Nordic Gem

  • Visit the stunning Helsinki Cathedral, a neoclassical landmark.

  • Explore the vibrant Market Square, famous for its local delicacies.

  • Take a ferry to the Suomenlinna Fortress, a UNESCO-listed sea fortress.

  • Experience modern Nordic design at the Oodi Library and Temppeliaukio Church (Rock Church).

5. Warsaw, Poland – A Historic Capital

  • Walk through the beautifully reconstructed Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

  • Visit the Royal Castle and the Lazienki Park, home to the famous Chopin Monument.

  • Learn about Polish history at the Warsaw Uprising Museum.

  • Stroll along Krakowskie Przedmieście, one of Warsaw’s most elegant streets.

We’ll travel through forests and plains to reach Parnu, a popular seaside resort. From there, we’ll continue to Latvia. You can enjoy an included lunch just before visiting the medieval Turaida Castle (admission included) surrounded by a large park with sculptures. Next, you can take a walk in Sigulda, a small and charming town situated amidst a beautiful landscape. Our journey will continue towards Riga, with a stop at the Salaspils Memorial, located at the site of a former concentration camp from the Nazi period. Since 1967, it has been one of the largest monumental complexes in Europe, commemorating the victims of this regime. We’ll arrive in Riga in the evening.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Vilnius ","to":"Warsaw","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":676,"h_name":"NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT","h_country":"Poland","h_city":"Warsaw","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT/1741095207837-IMG_20210905_074940_edited.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT/1741095207938-2020-11-07.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT/1741095207946-unnamed (39).jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Warsaw city tour ","day_summary":"

We’ll start the day with a panoramic tour of Warsaw with a local guide. Home to nearly two million people, this political and economic hub of Poland boasts a blend of modern architecture and meticulously restored historic buildings. We’ll stroll through the charming old town, featuring many old buildings, the Royal Palace, and cobbled streets, all restored after their destruction in World War II and now declared a World Heritage Site. We’ll visit the Palace of Culture, a skyscraper gifted by the Soviet Union, reflect on the history of what was once the largest Jewish ghetto in Europe, and wander through Lazienki Park, home to the Chopin Monument.


You’ll have free time in the afternoon to visit museums, such as the National Museum and the Warsaw Rising Museum, or to explore the lively city center filled with shops and activities.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Gdansk","country":"Poland","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Going to Gdansk ","day_summary":"

After breakfast, we’ll depart for northern Poland. Our first stop will be Olsztynek, where we’ll visit the open-air Museum of Folk Architecture (admission included). Here, you can admire wooden houses, old mills, and a traditional church, making it feel like you’ve stepped back in time! We’ll then continue to the Elblag Canal, the longest navigable canal in Poland, where we’ll visit a museum located in an area where boats are hauled over land on rails to navigate elevation changes. Next, we’ll head to Malbork to visit its impressive castle (admission included, green route), one of the largest medieval castles in Europe, built in the 13th century by the Teutonic Order and now a UNESCO World Heritage Site. We’ll arrive in Gdansk—formerly known as Danzig—in the evening. The historic center is stunning and lively, and we highly recommend an evening stroll to experience its vibrant atmosphere.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Warsaw","to":"Gdansk","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":795,"h_name":"Novotel Gdańsk Centrum","h_country":"Poland","h_city":"Gdansk","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Gdansk/Novotel Gdańsk Centrum/1741591326488-2024-12-23.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Gdansk/Novotel Gdańsk Centrum/1741591326637-unnamed - 2025-03-10T124945.908.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Gdansk/Novotel Gdańsk Centrum/1741591326642-2024-07-01 (7).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Sopot","country":"Poland","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Setting off for Sopot ","day_summary":"

After breakfast, we’ll join our guide for a visit to the European Solidarity Centre, a striking contemporary building that commemorates the history of the Solidarity movement, which played a pivotal role in the fall of the Soviet Union. Highlights include the monument dedicated to the fallen workers. Next, we’ll include admission to the Museum of the Second World War. This impressive museum, opened in 2017, serves as a powerful reminder of the 3 to 6 million people who lost their lives in Poland during that tragic conflict. Afterward, we’ll have some time to explore the historic center before heading to Sopot, a charming seaside resort on the Baltic Sea. Known for its vibrant atmosphere, Sopot features a long sandy beach and one of Europe’s longest wooden piers, offering stunning views of the sea. In the evening, we’ll board a modern ferry (cruise-style) for our journey to Sweden. The scheduled departure is at 07:30 p.m., with overnight accommodations in cabins. Breakfast will be included

Stockholm, the capital of Sweden, is a beautiful city spread across 14 islands connected by bridges, blending historic charm with modern innovation. The medieval Old Town, Gamla Stan, features narrow cobblestone streets, colorful buildings, and landmarks like the Royal Palace. The city is renowned for its museums, including the Vasa Museum, ABBA Museum, and Skansen, as well as its vibrant art scene with metro stations known as underground galleries. Stockholm boasts a strong focus on sustainability, clean urban planning, and a rich café culture centered around the Swedish tradition of fika. Each district offers its own vibe, from the trendy streets of Södermalm to the upscale elegance of Östermalm. Surrounded by the Stockholm archipelago, the city also provides stunning natural escapes just a boat ride away. With friendly English-speaking locals, excellent public transport, and a peaceful atmosphere, Stockholm is an ideal destination for culture, nature, and design lovers alike.

packages.package_name: A Grand Journey Through Northern Europe Stockholm to Gdansk
packages.overview:
• Stockholm, Sweden – The Venice of the North

  • Copenhagen, Denmark – A Fairytale City

    • Discover the Gamla Stan (Old Town) with its medieval charm and colorful buildings.

    • Visit the Royal Palace and witness the changing of the guard.

    • Explore the Vasa Museum, home to a remarkably preserved 17th-century warship.

    • Enjoy a scenic boat ride through the Stockholm Archipelago.

  • Berlin, Germany – A City of History & Modernity

    • Stroll along Nyhavn, a picturesque waterfront lined with vibrant buildings and cozy cafés.

    • Visit the famous Little Mermaid Statue and explore Tivoli Gardens, one of the world’s oldest amusement parks.

    • Discover the Rosenborg Castle, home to Denmark’s crown jewels.

    • Experience the bicycle-friendly culture of this dynamic city.

  • Gdansk, Poland – A Baltic Treasure

    • Wander through the beautifully restored Long Market (Dlugi Targ) and admire the colorful facades.

    • Visit the Neptune Fountain and the imposing St. Mary’s Church, one of the largest brick churches in the world.

    • Learn about World War II history at the Museum of the Second World War.

    • Experience the lively waterfront at Motlawa River Embankment, lined with charming cafes and historic buildings.

    • Walk through the iconic Brandenburg Gate and the historic Reichstag Building.

    • Reflect at the Memorial to the Murdered Jews of Europe and visit the Berlin Wall Memorial.

    • Explore the vibrant districts of Kreuzberg and Mitte, filled with art, culture, and nightlife.

    • Discover Museum Island, a UNESCO World Heritage site housing world-class museums.

Take some time to relax and settle in, soaking in the first impressions of your destination. If you have any questions or need assistance, our Travelxploria representative is always ready to help. In the afternoon, be sure to check the hotel reception area, where you’ll find important details about the start of your tour.

Today we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of this beautiful capital city, built on 14 interconnected islands. We’ll visit Gamla Stan, also known as the “the city between bridges”, the lively old quarter filled with monumental buildings such as the Nobel Prize Museum, located right beside the main square of Stortorget which honors Nobel Prize winners, the Cathedral, and the Royal Palace. Whenever possible, we’ll enjoy the stunning views from the Fjällgatan viewpoint. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore. We recommend visiting the museums and parks. Optionally, you can visit the City Hall and the Vasa Museum.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Leisure Day at Stockholm & Evening Boarding to Turku Ferry ","day_summary":"

Enjoy a free day in Stockholm. In the evening, we’ll board a modern ferry for an overnight journey to Finland. Accommodation will be in double en-suite cabins. 

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Helsinki","country":"Finland","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Finland/Helsinki/1742147811227-AdobeStock_56312148-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Finland/Helsinki/1742147812583-AdobeStock_246660981-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Finland/Helsinki/1742147812722-AdobeStock_306012807-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Finland/Helsinki/1742147812834-AdobeStock_422497458-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Stockholm to Helsinki","day_summary":"

As we arrive in Finland, we recommend heading to the ship’s deck for sunrise to enjoy the mesmerizing landscapes as we sail past the Åland Islands, a collection of hundreds of forested islands. After disembarking the ferry, you’ll have time to explore Turku, a city with a strong Swedish influence. We’ll then continue our journey to the neighboring city of Naantali, a picturesque town with charming wooden houses by the sea. Our travels will take us to Helsinki, where we’ll arrive around lunchtime. In the afternoon, you’ll have leisure time to explore the city, including its old market, Russian-influenced buildings, and beautiful churches.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Stockholm","to":"Helsinki","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":673,"h_name":"SCANDIC PARK HELSINKI","h_country":"Finland","h_city":"Helsinki","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Helsinki/SCANDIC PARK HELSINKI/1741094840842-unnamed (33).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Helsinki/SCANDIC PARK HELSINKI/1741094840919-unnamed (32).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Helsinki/SCANDIC PARK HELSINKI/1741094840959-unnamed (31).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Tallinn","country":"Estonia","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Helsinki to Tallinn","day_summary":"

We’ll embark on an included boat trip to the Suomenlinna Islands, six islands home to a magnificent fortress that once protected the city’s entrance and is now a World Heritage Site. Take some time to explore, crossing the islands via pedestrian bridges. In the afternoon, we’ll board a ferry to Estonia, a journey of about 2 hours and 30 minutes. As we depart from Helsinki, you’ll enjoy stunning views of the surrounding islands. In the evening, we’ll arrive in Tallinn.

The capital of Estonia is a wonderful city that has preserved its medieval core intact. You´ll enjoy a panoramic tour of the city, its walls, churches, squares, and its medieval streets filled with tourist attractions. Free time in the afternoon.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Riga","country":"Latvia","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Tallinn to Riga","day_summary":"

We’ll travel through forests and plains to reach Parnu, a popular seaside resort. From there, we’ll continue to Latvia. You can enjoy an included lunch just before visiting the medieval Turaida Castle (admission included) surrounded by a large park with sculptures. Next, you can take a walk in Sigulda, a small and charming town situated amidst a beautiful landscape. Our journey will continue towards Riga, with a stop at the Salaspils Memorial, located at the site of a former concentration camp from the Nazi period. Since 1967, it has been one of the largest monumental complexes in Europe, commemorating the victims of this regime. We’ll arrive in Riga in the evening.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Tallinn","to":"Riga","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":794,"h_name":"Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga","h_country":"Latvia","h_city":"Riga","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Riga/Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga/1741590631350-2025-02-23 (1).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Riga/Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga/1741590631476-2024-07-01 (6).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Riga/Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga/1741590631519-2021-06-14.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"City Tour at Riga","day_summary":"

You’ll enjoy an included panoramic tour of this capital, the most populous city in the Baltic states, where traces of the Soviet era are still visible. Riga is renowned not only for its cultural center but also for its medieval Historic Center and Art Nouveau architecture. The afternoon will be free for you to explore at your leisure.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Vilnius ","country":"Lithuania","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Riga to Vilnius ","day_summary":"

We’ll depart for Lithuania early in the morning. On our way there, we’ll visit the Baroque Palace of Rundale, built by the same architect who designed the Saint Petersburg’s Hermitage Museum. We’ll cross over into Lithuania. This country has a strong Catholic influence, highlighted by the impressive Hill of Crosses, with thousands of crosses of all sizes. Lunch will be included along our route. Afterwards, we’ll visit Trakai, a gorgeous castle located on an island in a lake, close to Vilnius. We’ll finish the day in Vilnius.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Riga","to":"Vilnius ","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":796,"h_name":"Radisson Blu Hotel Lietuva, Vilnius","h_country":"Lithuania","h_city":"Vilnius ","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Vilnius /Radisson Blu Hotel Lietuva, Vilnius/1744116855188-images (61).jpeg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Vilnius /Radisson Blu Hotel Lietuva, Vilnius/1744116855188-402086385.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Vilnius /Radisson Blu Hotel Lietuva, Vilnius/1744116855265-ice-344120-56185369-329725.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Vilnius City Tour","day_summary":"

We’ll begin with a panoramic tour of the city built on seven hills, often compared to Rome. You’ll discover its Baroque churches, historic walls, grand castle, bustling commercial streets in the center, and 1,200 medieval houses. Enjoy free time in the afternoon to explore at your leisure.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Warsaw","country":"Poland","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Vilnius to Warsaw","day_summary":"

We’ll continue our journey to Poland. Our first stop will be Grutas Park, also known as “Stalin’s World,” which features 86 statues from the Soviet era. We’ll then stop for lunch in the charming lakeside town of Augustów. Afterward, we’ll head to central Poland, arriving in Warsaw by the end of the day.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Vilnius ","to":"Warsaw","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":676,"h_name":"NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT","h_country":"Poland","h_city":"Warsaw","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT/1741095207837-IMG_20210905_074940_edited.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT/1741095207938-2020-11-07.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT/1741095207946-unnamed (39).jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Warsaw Tour","day_summary":"

We’ll start the day with a panoramic tour of Warsaw with a local guide. Home to nearly two million people, this political and economic hub of Poland boasts a blend of modern architecture and meticulously restored historic buildings. We’ll stroll through the charming old town, featuring many old buildings, the Royal Palace, and cobbled streets, all restored after their destruction in World War II and now declared a World Heritage Site. We’ll visit the Palace of Culture, a skyscraper gifted by the Soviet Union, reflect on the history of what was once the largest Jewish ghetto in Europe, and wander through Lazienki Park, home to the Chopin Monument.


You’ll have free time in the afternoon to visit museums, such as the National Museum and the Warsaw Rising Museum, or to explore the lively city center filled with shops and activities.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Gdansk","country":"Poland","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Warsaw to Gdansk","day_summary":"

After breakfast, we’ll depart for northern Poland. Our first stop will be Olsztynek, where we’ll visit the open-air Museum of Folk Architecture (admission included). Here, you can admire wooden houses, old mills, and a traditional church, making it feel like you’ve stepped back in time! We’ll then continue to the Elblag Canal, the longest navigable canal in Poland, where we’ll visit a museum located in an area where boats are hauled over land on rails to navigate elevation changes. Next, we’ll head to Malbork to visit its impressive castle (admission included, green route), one of the largest medieval castles in Europe, built in the 13th century by the Teutonic Order and now a UNESCO World Heritage Site. We’ll arrive in Gdansk—formerly known as Danzig—in the evening. The historic center is stunning and lively, and we highly recommend an evening stroll to experience its vibrant atmosphere.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Warsaw","to":"Gdansk","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":795,"h_name":"Novotel Gdańsk Centrum","h_country":"Poland","h_city":"Gdansk","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Gdansk/Novotel Gdańsk Centrum/1741591326488-2024-12-23.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Gdansk/Novotel Gdańsk Centrum/1741591326637-unnamed - 2025-03-10T124945.908.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Gdansk/Novotel Gdańsk Centrum/1741591326642-2024-07-01 (7).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Sopot","country":"Poland","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Sopot Day Tour & Nynäshamn Ferry at Evening to Proceed Towards Stockholm","day_summary":"

After breakfast, we’ll join our guide for a visit to the European Solidarity Centre, a striking contemporary building that commemorates the history of the Solidarity movement, which played a pivotal role in the fall of the Soviet Union. Highlights include the monument dedicated to the fallen workers. Next, we’ll include admission to the Museum of the Second World War. This impressive museum, opened in 2017, serves as a powerful reminder of the 3 to 6 million people who lost their lives in Poland during that tragic conflict. Afterward, we’ll have some time to explore the historic center before heading to Sopot, a charming seaside resort on the Baltic Sea. Known for its vibrant atmosphere, Sopot features a long sandy beach and one of Europe’s longest wooden piers, offering stunning views of the sea. In the evening, we’ll board a modern ferry (cruise-style) for our journey to Sweden. The scheduled departure is at 07:30 p.m., with overnight accommodations in cabins. Breakfast will be included.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Gdansk","to":"Sopot","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":852,"h_name":"Radisson Blu Hotel","h_country":"Poland","h_city":"Sopot","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Sopot/Radisson Blu Hotel/1741694640748-Swimming pool - Radisson Blu Sopot.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Sopot/Radisson Blu Hotel/1741694640904-2023-12-31.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Sopot/Radisson Blu Hotel/1741694640911-2023-08-07.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Stockholm Sightseeing & Departure","day_summary":"

After a pleasant journey, our ferry will arrive in Karlskrona in southern Sweden around 06:30 a.m. We’ll continue to Stockholm, with a stop in Västervik, a charming seaside town with a picturesque historic center. In the afternoon, we’ll arrive in Stockholm, the capital of Sweden, a beautiful city spread across fourteen islands in the Baltic Sea archipelago, connected by numerous bridges and ferries. Stockholm is renowned for its rich history, stunning architecture, and its blend of modernity and tradition. The afternoon is yours to enjoy. We recommend taking a stroll through the historic center and visiting its museums and parks.Our journey will come to an end, leaving you with wonderful and lasting memories.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Sopot","to":"Stockholm","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Stockholm","to":"Stockholm Arlanda Airport","splitStops":[]}}]}]}]

packages.package_name: Baltics Wonders: From Stockholm to Warsaw
packages.overview:
• Stockholm – Explore Gamla Stan’s medieval charm, visit the Nobel Prize Museum, and admire the Royal Palace’s grandeur.
  • Overnight Ferry to Turku – Sail through the stunning Åland Islands and wake up to Finland’s scenic beauty.
  • Helsinki & Naantali – Stroll through Helsinki’s vibrant markets and Naantali’s picturesque seaside wooden houses.
  • Tallinn – Walk Tallinn’s enchanting medieval streets, surrounded by ancient city walls and historic landmarks.
  • Riga – Discover Riga’s Art Nouveau wonders and Soviet-era history through its captivating architecture.
  • Vilnius & Trakai – Admire Vilnius’ baroque skyline and visit Trakai Castle, an island fortress steeped in history.
  • Warsaw – Experience Warsaw’s rebuilt Old Town, historic ghetto memorials, and the grandeur of Lazienki Park.

​Today we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of this beautiful capital city, built on 14 interconnected islands. We’ll visit Gamla Stan, also known as the “the city between bridges”, the lively old quarter filled with monumental buildings such as the Nobel Prize Museum, located right beside the main square of Stortorget which honors Nobel Prize winners, the Cathedral, and the Royal Palace. Whenever possible, we’ll enjoy the stunning views from the Fjällgatan viewpoint. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore. We recommend visiting the museums and parks. Optionally, you can visit the City Hall and the Vasa Museum.

​

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"Stockholm","to":"Helsinki","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":674,"h_name":"Scandic Grand Marina","h_country":"Finland","h_city":"Helsinki","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Helsinki/Scandic Grand Marina/1741094943349-unnamed (36).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Helsinki/Scandic Grand Marina/1741094943442-unnamed (35).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Helsinki/Scandic Grand Marina/1741094943485-unnamed (34).jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Visit to the Turku, Naantali, Helsinki","day_summary":"

As we arrive in Finland, we recommend heading to the ship’s deck for sunrise to enjoy the mesmerizing landscapes as we sail past the Åland Islands, a collection of hundreds of forested islands. After disembarking the ferry, you’ll have time to explore Turku, a city with a strong Swedish influence. We’ll then continue our journey to the neighboring city of Naantali, a picturesque town with charming wooden houses by the sea. Our travels will take us to Helsinki, where we’ll arrive around lunchtime. In the afternoon, you’ll have leisure time to explore the city, including its old market, Russian-influenced buildings, and beautiful churches.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Tallinn","country":"Estonia","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Experiencing Helsinki in Morning & Afternoon Transfer to Tallinn","day_summary":"

We’ll embark on an included boat trip to the Suomenlinna Islands, six islands home to a magnificent fortress that once protected the city’s entrance and is now a World Heritage Site. Take some time to explore, crossing the islands via pedestrian bridges. In the afternoon, we’ll board a ferry to Estonia, a journey of about 2 hours and 30 minutes. As we depart from Helsinki, you’ll enjoy stunning views of the surrounding islands. In the evening, we’ll arrive in Tallinn. 

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"Helsinki","to":"Tallinn","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":792,"h_name":"Park Inn by Radisson Meriton Conference & Spa Hotel Tallinn","h_country":"Estonia","h_city":"Tallinn","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Tallinn/Park Inn by Radisson Meriton Conference & Spa Hotel Tallinn/1741590256805-2024-10-27.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Tallinn/Park Inn by Radisson Meriton Conference & Spa Hotel Tallinn/1741590256895-unnamed - 2025-03-10T123217.824.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Tallinn/Park Inn by Radisson Meriton Conference & Spa Hotel Tallinn/1741590256896-2024-05-16 (2).jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"City Tour of Tallinn","day_summary":"

The capital of Estonia is a wonderful city that has preserved its medieval core intact. You´ll enjoy a panoramic tour of the city, its walls, churches, squares, and its medieval streets filled with tourist attractions. Free time in the afternoon.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Riga","country":"Latvia","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Departure from Tallinn & Arrival at Riga","day_summary":"

We’ll travel through forests and plains to reach Parnu, a popular seaside resort. From there, we’ll continue to Latvia. You can enjoy an included lunch just before visiting the medieval Turaida Castle (admission included) surrounded by a large park with sculptures. Next, you can take a walk in Sigulda, a small and charming town situated amidst a beautiful landscape. Our journey will continue towards Riga, with a stop at the Salaspils Memorial, located at the site of a former concentration camp from the Nazi period. Since 1967, it has been one of the largest monumental complexes in Europe, commemorating the victims of this regime. We’ll arrive in Riga in the evening.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"Tallinn","to":"Riga","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":794,"h_name":"Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga","h_country":"Latvia","h_city":"Riga","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Riga/Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga/1741590631350-2025-02-23 (1).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Riga/Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga/1741590631476-2024-07-01 (6).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Riga/Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga/1741590631519-2021-06-14.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"City Tour at Riga","day_summary":"

You’ll enjoy an included panoramic tour of this capital, the most populous city in the Baltic states, where traces of the Soviet era are still visible. Riga is renowned not only for its cultural center but also for its medieval Historic Center and Art Nouveau architecture. The afternoon will be free for you to explore at your leisure.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Vilnius ","country":"Lithuania","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Riga Departure & Arrival at Vilnius","day_summary":"

We’ll depart for Lithuania early in the morning. On our way there, we’ll visit the Baroque Palace of Rundale, built by the same architect who designed the Saint Petersburg’s Hermitage Museum. We’ll cross over into Lithuania. This country has a strong Catholic influence, highlighted by the impressive Hill of Crosses, with thousands of crosses of all sizes. Lunch will be included along our route. Afterwards, we’ll visit Trakai, a gorgeous castle located on an island in a lake, close to Vilnius. We’ll finish the day in Vilnius.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Riga","to":"Vilnius ","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":796,"h_name":"Radisson Blu Hotel Lietuva, Vilnius","h_country":"Lithuania","h_city":"Vilnius ","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Vilnius City Tour","day_summary":"

We’ll begin with a panoramic tour of the city built on seven hills, often compared to Rome. You’ll discover its Baroque churches, historic walls, grand castle, bustling commercial streets in the center, and 1,200 medieval houses. Enjoy free time in the afternoon to explore at your leisure.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Warsaw","country":"Poland","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Vilnius Departure & Arrival at Warsaw","day_summary":"

We’ll continue our journey to Poland. Our first stop will be Grutas Park, also known as “Stalin’s World,” which features 86 statues from the Soviet era. We’ll then stop for lunch in the charming lakeside town of Augustów. Afterward, we’ll head to central Poland, arriving in Warsaw by the end of the day.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Vilnius ","to":"Warsaw","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":675,"h_name":"RADISSON BLU SOBIESKI","h_country":"Poland","h_city":"Warsaw","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/RADISSON BLU SOBIESKI/1741095111105-unnamed (38).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/RADISSON BLU SOBIESKI/1741095111174-unnamed (37).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/RADISSON BLU SOBIESKI/1741095111205-2021-10-21.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Warsaw Sightseeing & Departure in the Evening","day_summary":"

We’ll start the day with a panoramic tour of Warsaw with a local guide. Home to nearly two million people, this political and economic hub of Poland boasts a blend of modern architecture and meticulously restored historic buildings. We’ll stroll through the charming Old Town, featuring many old buildings, the Royal Palace, and cobbled streets, all restored after their destruction in World War II and now declared a World Heritage Site. We’ll visit the Palace of Culture, a skyscraper gifted by the Soviet Union, reflect on the history of what was once the largest Jewish ghetto in Europe, and wander through Lazienki Park, home to the Chopin Monument.



You’ll have free time in the afternoon to visit museums, such as the National Museum and the Warsaw Rising Museum, or to explore the lively city center filled with shops and activities.



Our journey will come to an end, leaving you with wonderful and lasting memories.

Please, check the time of your flight in case you might need an additional night.

overview:
Best India to Europe Tour Package – Discover the Continent’s Timeless Charm

Best India to Europe tour package experiences with Travelxploria open the gateway to fairy-tale cities, breathtaking landscapes, and centuries of history. From the romantic streets of Paris to the magical canals of Venice, our curated packages bring you the best of Europe with luxury, comfort, and local expertise. Whether you’re traveling solo, as a couple, or with family, our itineraries are designed to turn your Europe dream into reality. Explore our full range of Tour Package India options for seamless bookings.

Why Book the Best India to Europe Tour Package?

Iconic Cities and Scenic Landscapes
Our Europe tour package takes you to destinations you’ve always dreamed of—London, Paris, Rome, Amsterdam, and more. Every trip blends cultural highlights with free time to explore at your own pace. You can also check out specialized uk tour packages from india or explore scenic northern routes with our finland tour package from india.

Convenient Departures from Major Indian Cities
With Europe tour packages from Mumbai, Europe tour package from Delhi, Europe tour package from Kolkata, and Europe tour packages from Bangalore, your European getaway starts close to home. For more exotic experiences, consider adding a stop with our jordan tour packages from india or turkey tour packages from india.

Perfect for First-Time and Repeat Travelers
From 7 days Europe tour packages from India to extended journeys, we cater to every schedule. Whether you prefer a classic circuit or an Eastern Europe tour package with Prague, Budapest, and Kraków, our routes are crafted for maximum value. Families can enjoy tailored family tour packages while honeymooners may prefer our specially curated honeymoon packages. For solo explorers, check out our solo travel packages or for thrill-seekers, our adventure tour packages.

Top Itineraries in Our Best India to Europe Tour Package

7 Days Europe Tour Packages from India
Perfect for travelers on a tight schedule—covering Paris, Lucerne, and Rome with guided sightseeing and leisure days.

Eastern Europe Tour Package
Explore the charm of Eastern Europe with visits to Vienna, Budapest, Prague, and Warsaw, featuring scenic train rides and cultural tours. Consider complementing your trip with the uzbekistan tour package from india for an enriching extension.

Luxury Europe Tour Package from India
Enjoy private transfers, premium hotels, and exclusive experiences like gourmet dining in Paris or a private gondola ride in Venice.

Popular Destinations Covered
Paris – Eiffel Tower, Louvre Museum, and Seine River cruise.
Rome – Colosseum, Vatican City, and timeless piazzas.
Amsterdam – Canal tours and Van Gogh Museum.
Prague – Charles Bridge, Old Town, and historic castles in our Eastern Europe tour package.

Explore more northern delights with our sweden tour packages from india or denmark tour package from india.

What’s Included in the Best India to Europe Tour Package?

1. Round-trip airfare from India.
2. Accommodation in premium 3★/4★ hotels with breakfast.
3. Guided sightseeing in major cities.
4. All transfers and intercity travel by coach or high-speed train.

Optional add-ons like a Europe tour package with river cruise on the Seine or Rhine.


Best Time to Visit Europe

Spring (April–June) – Mild weather, blooming landscapes, and fewer crowds.
Autumn (September–October) – Golden scenery and pleasant travel conditions.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Do you offer an affordable India to Europe tour package?
Yes. Our affordable India to Europe tour package options include handpicked hotels, economy flights, and smart itineraries to keep costs low without sacrificing experiences.

Q2: What is included in an India to Europe tour package with sightseeing?
Our India to Europe tour package with sightseeing includes guided city tours, entrance tickets to major attractions, and leisure time for personal exploration.

Q3: Is there an India to Europe tour package with river cruise?
Absolutely. We offer an India to Europe tour package with river cruise, such as the Seine in Paris or Rhine in Germany, as part of select itineraries.

Q4: Which is the best budget India to Europe tour package?
Our best budget India to Europe tour package combines multi-city sightseeing, comfortable stays, and shared transfers, offering maximum value for money.

Q5: Can I depart from my home city?
Yes. We offer departures for Europe tour packages from Mumbai, Europe tour package from Delhi, Europe tour package from Kolkata, and Europe tour packages from Bangalore with customized flight options.
With Travelxploria’s best India to Europe tour package, you get more than a vacation—you get a crafted journey through the heart of Europe. From classic landmarks to hidden gems, our packages, including Eastern Europe tour package options, promise experiences that will stay with you forever.
